THE NARROWING OF THE JJ ACTS LIKE AN OBSTRUCTION AND THEREFORE THE LEAK IN THE ANASTOMOSIS IS SECONDARY TO THE ELEVATED PRESSURE CAUSE BY THE OBSTRUCTION. THE NARROWED JJ ANASTOMOSIS IS SECONDARY TO AN ERROR IN SURGICAL TECHNIQUE. ADDITIONALLY, THE FOLLOWING INFORMATION WAS PROVIDED BY THE ISI MEDICAL SAFETY OFFICER: FROM THE STAPLER LOGS IT IS LIKELY THAT THE SURGEON USED THE TWO GREEN AND ONE OR TWO BLUE STAPLE CARTRIDGES TO CREATE THE GASTRIC POUCH. IF THE SURGEON USED BOTH GREEN AND BOTH BLUE CARTRIDGES ON THE GASTRIC POUCH, THEN THE GJ ANASTOMOSIS WAS HAND SEWN. IF THE SURGEON USED ONLY ONE BLUE CARTRIDGE ON THE GASTRIC POUCH, THEN THE GJ WAS FORMED PARTIALLY BY A BLUE CARTRIDGE FOLLOWED BY THE HAND-SEWN COMPLETION OF THE COMMON DEFECT. THE THREE WHITE LOADS WERE USED TO FIRST DIVIDE THE SMALL BOWEL SEPARATING THE ROUX LIMB FROM THE BPD LIMB. ONE WHITE LOAD WAS USED TO FORM THE JJ ANASTOMOSIS. THE THIRD AND FINAL WHITE LOAD WAS USED TO CLOSE THE COMMON DEFECT OF THE JJ. IT WAS INITIALLY REPORTED THAT DURING A DA VINCI-ASSISTED VENTRAL HERNIA REPAIR PROCEDURE, THE SURGEON WAS OPTI-VIEWING WITH A TROCAR AND IT KEPT SHOWING OCCLUSION; THERE WAS A LITTLE BIT OF BLOOD BUT WASN'T MUCH. THE SURGEON PLACED THE TROCAR IN A DIFFERENT LOCATION DUE TO THE OCCLUSION AND FINISHED PLACING PORTS AND DOCKED ROBOT. THE PATIENT'S BLOOD PRESSURE DROPPED SEVERELY AND THE SYSTEM WAS UNDOCKED. IT WAS REPORTED THAT THE SURGEON HAD HIT THE AORTA WITH THE TROCAR WHEN ORIGINALLY TRYING TO PLACE IT. THE CASE STARTED AT 10:00 AM AND PATIENT EXPIRED AROUND 2:00 PM. A COUPLE OF OTHER SURGEONS CAME IN THE ROOM TO TRY TO HELP REVIVE PATIENT AND WERE UNSUCCESSFUL. THE PATIENT SUBSEQUENTLY EXPIRED. ON 25-FEB-2021, INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC. (ISI) OBTAINED THE FOLLOWING ADDITIONAL INFORMATION REGARDING THE REPORTED EVENT: DURING A DA VINCI-ASSISTED VENTRAL HERNIA REPAIR PROCEDURE ON (B)(6) 2021, A (B)(6) FEMALE PATIENT EXPIRED POST PLACEMENT OF A TROCAR WHICH ALLEGEDLY CAUSED A LACERATION TO THE AORTA AND RESULTED IN EXCESSIVE BLEEDING. IT WAS REPORTED THAT AS THE SURGEON WAS ATTEMPTING TO INSERT AN OPTIVIEW 5MM TROCAR" BY APPLIED MEDICAL WITH A 5MM "STRYKER SCOPE, THE SURGEON MET WITH AN OCCLUSION. IT WAS REPORTED THAT, INSTEAD OF PLACING IT ON THE SIDE, THE SURGEON WENT FOR INGUINAL PLACEMENT ABOVE THE BELLY BUTTON. DUE TO THE OCCLUSION, THE SURGEON WENT TO THE OTHER SIDE AND GOT IN. THERE WAS AN UNSPECIFIED SMALL AMOUNT OF BLOOD OBSERVED AT AN UNKNOWN LOCATION AND PORT PLACEMENT AND SYSTEM DOCKING WAS COMPLETED. JUST AS THE SYSTEM DOCKED, THE PATIENT'S BLOOD PRESSURE DROPPED SEVERELY AND THE SYSTEM WAS UNDOCKED. THE PROCEDURE WAS CONVERTED TO OPEN SURGERY WHERE IT WAS OBSERVED THAT THE AORTA HAD BEEN DAMAGED; THE AORTA WAS PUNCTURED AT THE POINT WHERE THE INITIAL TROCAR PLACEMENT HAD BEEN ATTEMPTED. THERE WAS BLOOD LOSS OF WELL OVER 750 ML AND AN UNSPECIFIED AMOUNT OF BLOOD TRANSFUSION WAS ADMINISTERED. CONSULTING SURGEONS ATTEMPTED TO ASSIST WITH THE EVENT. IT WAS REPORTED THAT THE SITE TRIED EVERYTHING THEY COULD; WORKING ON THE PATIENT FROM 10:00 AM TO 2:00 PM YET SPECIFIC ATTEMPTED MEDICAL INTERVENTION AND SPECIFIC ADDITIONAL COMPLICATIONS WERE UNKNOWN. THE PATIENT SUBSEQUENTLY EXPIRED ON (B)(6) 2021.
A REVIEW OF THE INSTRUMENT LOGS WAS PERFORMED FOR A PROCEDURE ON (B)(6) 2021 ON SYSTEM (B)(4). THERE WERE FOUR INSTRUMENTS RECORDED FOR THIS PROCEDURE, THREE OF WHICH WERE RECORDED AS BEING USED DURING THIS PROCEDURE. 8MM 30 DEGREE ENDOSCOPE (PART #470027-65; LOT #0000903-289; SERIAL (B)(4)) HAD 1977 OF 2000 USES REMAINING AND WAS RECORDED AS BEING USED FOR 19 SECONDS IN THIS PROCEDURE. FENESTRATED BIPOLAR FORCEPS (FBF) INSTRUMENT (PART #471205-17; LOT #N14200615-0080; SERIAL (B)(4)) HAD 13 OF 14 USES REMAINING AND WAS RECORDED AS BEING USED FOR 54 SECONDS IN THIS PROCEDURE. PERMANENT CAUTERY HOOK (PCH) INSTRUMENT (PART #470183-14; LOT #N11200727-0032; SERIAL (B)(4)) HAD 9 OF 10 USES REMAINING AND WAS NOT RECORDED AS BEING USED FOR THIS PROCEDURE. SINGLE USE SUCTION IRRIGATOR (PART #480299-06; LOT #M10200510-0218; SERIAL (B)(4)) HAD 0 OF 1 USES REMAINING AND WAS RECORDED AS BEING USED FOR 49 SECONDS IN THIS PROCEDURE. WHILE ALL REUSABLE INSTRUMENTS USED IN THE CASE HAVE NOT BEEN USED IN SUBSEQUENT PROCEDURES AT THIS TIME, A SITE HISTORY SEARCH SHOWS NO COMPLAINTS FILED AGAINST THE INSTRUMENTS. THE MAXIMUM AMOUNT OF TIME THAT ANY INSTRUMENT WAS IN USE WAS 54 SECONDS. TROCARS ARE ACCESSORIES AND ARE NOT RECORDED IN SYSTEM LOGS. A SYSTEM ERROR LOG REVIEW WAS ALSO CONDUCTED. THERE WERE NO OBSERVED EVENTS IN THE SYSTEM LOGS THAT WOULD SUGGEST A PRODUCT ISSUE AND LOGGED EVENTS ARE IN LINE WITH NORMAL SYSTEM FUNCTIONALITY. THE LOGS SHOWED THAT THE PROCEDURE STARTED AND THE SURGEON WENT INTO FOLLOWING MODE APPROXIMATELY 3 MINUTES THEREAFTER. WHILE THE END OF PROCEDURE WAS RECORDED AT 212 MINUTES (3.5 HOURS) LATER, INSTRUMENTS SHOW IN USE FOR ONLY A MAXIMUM OF 54 SECONDS. NO IMAGE OR VIDEO CLIP FOR THE REPORTED EVENT WAS SUBMITTED FOR REVIEW. A MEDICAL REVIEW FROM AN ISI MEDICAL SAFETY OFFICER WAS COMPLETED AND RESULTS WERE AS FOLLOWS: BASED UPON THE INFORMATION IN THE DESCRIPTION OF THE EVENT, THE PATIENTS AORTA WAS INJURED DURING THE INITIAL PLACEMENT OF A THIRD-PARTY TROCAR RESULTING IN ACUTE BLOOD LOSS ANEMIA, LEADING TO HYPOPERFUSION AND SUBSEQUENT DEATH. THERE IS A CLEAR INDICATION FROM THE SITE THAT THE INJURY TO THE AORTA WAS CAUSED DURING THE INITIAL PLACEMENT OF THE THIRD-PARTY TROCAR. ADDITIONALLY, THE INFORMATION FROM THE SYSTEM LOGS WOULD INDICATE THAT BLEEDING WAS PRESENT WITHIN THE ABDOMEN PRIOR TO THE SURGEON STARTING THE ROBOTIC PORTION OF THE OPERATIVE PROCEDURE. THE DA VINCI SURGICAL SYSTEM, INSTRUMENTATION, AND ACCESSORIES WERE ONLY IN USE FOR A TOTAL OF 54 SECONDS. A SUCTION IRRIGATOR WAS USED IN 49 OF THOSE 54 SECONDS. IT IS HIGHLY IRREGULAR TO REQUIRE THE USE OF A SUCTION IRRIGATOR AT THE START OF ANY CASE, LET ALONE A VENTRAL HERNIA REPAIR. THE SUCTION IRRIGATOR WAS MOST LIKELY REQUIRED BY THE SURGEON OF RECORD TO HELP IDENTIFY THE SOURCE OF BLEEDING THAT OCCURRED FROM AN INJURY SUSTAINED TO THE AORTA FROM THE THIRD-PARTY TROCAR PRIOR TO EVEN DOCKING THE DA VINCI SYSTEM. THE DA VINCI SYSTEM, INSTRUMENTATION, AND ACCESSORIES DID NOT CAUSE OR CONTRIBUTE TO THE INJURY SUSTAINED TO THE PATIENTS AORTA WHEN THE SURGEON PLACED A THIRD-PARTY TROCAR CAUSING ACUTE BLOOD LOSS ANEMIA, HYPOPERFUSION, AND DEATH. THIS COMPLAINT IS BEING REPORTED DUE TO THE FOLLOWING CONCLUSION: DURING A DA VINCI-ASSISTED VENTRAL HERNIA REPAIR PROCEDURE, THE PATIENT EXPIRED DUE TO ACUTE BLOOD LOSS ANEMIA AND HYPOPERFUSION DUE TO AN AORTIC INJURY RESULTING FROM THE SURGEON PLACING A TROCAR. WHILE IT WAS REPORTED THAT THE LOCATION OF THE BLEEDING WAS AT THE LOCATION WHERE THE INITIAL TROCAR (A THIRD-PARTY MANUFACTURER PRODUCT) PLACEMENT WAS ATTEMPTED, IT IS UNKNOWN TO WHAT DEGREE THE DA VINCI SYSTEM, INSTRUMENTS, AND ACCESSORIES USED DURING THIS PROCEDURE MAY HAVE CAUSED OR CONTRIBUTED TO THE EVENT.

IT WAS REPORTED THAT DURING A DA VINCI-ASSISTED PULMONARY LOBECTOMY PROCEDURE, THE SURGEON WAS TAKING DOWN SCAR TISSUE ON THE PULMONARY VEIN, WHEN IT WAS OBSERVED THAT THE PULMONARY VEIN WAS NICKED, WHICH RESULTED IN BLEEDING. THE SURGEON APPLIED PRESSURE WITH AN UNSPECIFIED INSTRUMENT AND MADE THE DECISION TO CONVERT TO AN OPEN APPROACH. ONCE IT WAS DECIDED THAT THE PROCEDURE WAS TO BE CONVERTED TO OPEN, THE SURGEON REQUESTED THAT A SECOND BOVIE BE BROUGHT INTO THE OPERATING ROOM (OR), AND HE UNDOCKED THE ENDOSCOPE AND OTHER DOCKED SYSTEM ARMS WITH THE EXCEPTION OF ONE ARM THAT HAD AN UNSPECIFIED INSTRUMENT INSTALLED THAT WAS HOLDING THE VEIN TO CONTINUOUSLY APPLY PRESSURE. DESPITE THIS EFFORT TO HOLD THE VEIN WITH AN INSTRUMENT, THE VEIN CONTINUED TO OOZE. IT WAS THEN REPORTED THAT THE INTEGRATED ELECTROSURGICAL UNITS (IESU) POWER WAS INCREASED FROM A SETTING OF THREE TO EIGHT AND, AT THE SAME TIME, THE SECOND BOVIE THAT WAS PREVIOUSLY REQUESTED WAS PLUGGED INTO THE SAME OUTLET AS THE IESU. DURING THESE TWO ACTIONS, IT WAS REPORTED THAT THE OPERATING ROOM LOST POWER. WHILE IT WAS NOT CONFIRMED, THE SITE INDICATED THAT THEY BELIEVED THAT THE INCREASING OF THE IESU SETTING AND SIMULTANEOUS PLUGGING-IN OF THE BOVIE INTO THE SAME OUTLET COULD HAVE CAUSED THE POWER OUTAGE. AT THE TIME OF THE POWER OUTAGE, THE DA VINCI SURGICAL SYSTEMS PATIENT SIDE CART (PSC) WENT INTO DEFAULT BATTERY MODE. ARM 1 WAS THEN UNDOCKED AND IT WAS NOTED THAT IT WAS NOT IN THE SURGEONS WAY AS INITIALLY INDICATED BY THE RISK MANAGER. THE CONVERSION TO OPEN SURGEON CONTINUED FOLLOWING THESE EVENTS AND THE BLEEDING WAS CONTROLLED WITH ELECTROCAUTERY PROVIDED BY THE IESU AND AN UNSPECIFIED ENERGY INSTRUMENT. THE ESTIMATED BLOOD LOSS WAS TWO LITERS AND THE PATIENT WAS TRANSFUSED WITH A COUPLE UNITS OF BLOOD. IT WAS NOTED THAT AFTER THE BLEEDING WAS CONTROLLED, THE PATIENT CODED AND COULD NOT BE REVIVED. IT WAS ALSO NOTED THAT THE SURGEON AT THE SITE BELIEVES THAT THE PATIENT COULD NOT TOLERATE THE BLOOD LOSS DUE TO THE PATIENTS CONDITIONS AND COMORBIDITIES. THERE WAS NO AUTOPSY PERFORMED, BUT THE CAUSE OF DEATH WAS NOTED TO BE HEMORRHAGIC SHOCK WITH CONTRIBUTING FACTORS BEING BLEEDING AND A LUNG MASS. THE RISK MANAGER THEN CONFIRMED THAT NEITHER THE SITE NOR SURGEON HAVE ALLEGED A MALFUNCTION OF AN ISI DEVICE OR SYSTEM CAUSED OR CONTRIBUTED TO THE PULMONARY VEIN INJURY OR PATIENTS DEATH. THE RISK MANAGER STATED THAT THE SURGEON CONFIRMED THAT THE NICKING OF THE VEIN WAS NOTED TO BE A KNOWN SURGICAL COMPLICATION AND NOT A RESULT OF THE INSTRUMENTATION. AN INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC. (ISI) REPRESENTATIVE WAS PRESENT FOR THE EVENT AND NOTED THAT THE UNSPECIFIED INSTRUMENT THAT WAS USED TO HOLD THE VEIN WAS A CADIERE FORCEPS INSTRUMENT. WITH REGARD TO THE RISK MANAGERS SPEAKING TO AN INITIAL ALLEGATION OF A SYSTEM ARM BEING IN THE WAY, A NURSE REPORTED A COMPLAINT TO ISI FOR THE REPORTED EVENT AND INDICATED THAT AN ARM, UNIVERSAL SURGICAL MANIPULATOR (USM) 2, THAT HAD BEEN UNDOCKED FROM THE PATIENT WAS IN THE SURGEONS WAY. THE NURSE INDICATED THAT AN ERROR, UNABLE TO MOVE ARM NUMBER 2, HAD APPEARED ON THE SCREEN.

IT WAS REPORTED THAT AN (B)(6) FEMALE EXPIRED TWO DAYS AFTER A DA VINCI-ASSISTED SIGMOID COLECTOMY WITH COLO-ANAL ANASTOMOSIS SURGICAL PROCEDURE, WHICH WAS PERFORMED ON (B)(6) 2020. THE PATIENT WAS ILL PRIOR TO THE PROCEDURE, AND THE PATIENT EXPIRED SECONDARY TO PULMONARY AND CARDIAC FAILURE. ON (B)(6) 2020, INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC. (ISI) CONTACTED THE INITIAL REPORTER, AN ISI REPRESENTATIVE, AND HE STATED THAT HE OBTAINED THE INFORMATION OF THE PATIENT'S DEATH FROM THE SURGEON ON (B)(6) 2020. THE PATIENT EXPIRED TWO DAYS POST-OPERATIVELY, ON (B)(6) 2020, SECONDARY TO PULMONARY AND CARDIAC FAILURE. IT WAS CONFIRMED THAT THERE WERE NO INTRA-OPERATIVE COMPLICATIONS DURING THE ROBOTIC PROCEDURE, AND NO ALLEGATION THAT A MALFUNCTION OF A DA VINCI SYSTEM, INSTRUMENT, OR ACCESSORY OCCURRED. ON (B)(6) 2020, UPON ADDITIONAL FOLLOW-UP, THE INITIAL REPORTER OBTAINED THE FOLLOWING INFORMATION FROM THE SURGEON: THE PATIENT HAD A COLOVESICAL FISTULA. THE PATIENT WAS REPORTED TO BE ILL PRIOR TO THE PROCEDURE. THE SURGEON HAD REPORTED THAT THERE WAS MINIMAL BLOOD LOSS OF 25 CC DURING THE PROCEDURE. THE PATIENT CODED AT 7:00 AM ON (B)(6) 2020 AFTER BEING FOUND UNRESPONSIVE. NO EPISODES OF TACHYCARDIA OR HYPOXIA WERE PREVIOUSLY REPORTED. THE PATIENT HAD "TRANSTHORACIC ECHO" AFTER CODING WHICH WERE CONSISTENT WITH "TAKOTSUBO CARDIOMYOPATHY." EKG SHOWED ST ELEVATIONS. A CHEST X-RAY SHOWED VASCULAR CONGESTION. AN ICU PHYSICIAN DIAGNOSED "TAKOTSUBO CARDIOMYOPATHY" AND INITIATED MYOCARDIAL INFARCTION (MI) PROTOCOL ON (B)(6) 2020. CARE WAS WITHDRAWN ON (B)(6)2020. BASED ON THE SYSTEM LOG REVIEW, IT WAS IDENTIFIED THAT THE MANUAL RELEASE KNOB (MRK), THE INSTRUMENT'S GRIP RELEASE TOOL, WAS USED ON THE SUREFORM 60 STAPLER INSTRUMENT. THE SURGEON, WHEN ASKED ABOUT THE USE OF THE MRK, REPORTED THE FOLLOWING: THERE WAS A NEW OR TECH IN THE ROOM WHO TRIED TO RELEASE THE SUREFORM 60 STAPLER INSTRUMENT FROM THE INSTRUMENT ARM. HE STATED THAT THE SUREFORM 60 STAPLER INSTRUMENT WOULD NOT RELEASE AND THE OR TECH STARTED TO TURN THE MRK. THE STAPLER INSTRUMENT WAS NOT GRASPING ANY TISSUE AT THE TIME. IT WAS CONFIRMED THAT THERE WERE NO INTRA-OPERATIVE COMPLICATIONS DURING THE ROBOTIC PROCEDURE, AND NO ALLEGATION THAT A MALFUNCTION OF A DA VINCI SYSTEM, INSTRUMENT, OR ACCESSORY OCCURRED. AS A RESULT, THE SINGLE USE INSTRUMENTS AND ACCESSORIES WERE DISCARDED.
IF ADDITIONAL INFORMATION IS RECEIVED, A FOLLOW-UP MDR WILL BE SUBMITTED. A REVIEW OF THE SYSTEM AND INSTRUMENT LOGS WAS PERFORMED. THE SUREFORM 60 STAPLER INSTRUMENT WAS USED AND FIRED TWO GREEN RELOADS. BOTH FIRINGS WERE COMPLETED PER THE LOGS WITH NO PAUSES FOR COMPRESSION. THERE WERE NO INCOMPLETE CLAMPS IN THE PROCEDURE. BESIDES THE GRIP RELEASE TOOL DETECTION, THERE WAS ONE INSTANCE OF A REMOTE CENTER MONITOR EVENT THAT INDICATES DURING TABLE MOTION, THE MOVEMENT OF ARM 1 HIT THE LIMIT AND THUS TABLE MOTION WAS STOPPED. THERE WERE NO FURTHER OCCURRENCES OF THIS BEHAVIOR. ROUGHLY 40 SECONDS AFTER THE LAST UNCLAMP EVENT, THE SYSTEM LOGS SHOW AN ERROR ASSOCIATED WITH THE SUREFORM INSTRUMENT INDICATING THAT THE TOOL WAS FORCE EXPIRED. FORCE EXPIRATION IS THE EXPECTED SYSTEM BEHAVIOR WHEN THE MRK IS USED. THE FOLLOWING ERRORS ARE ALSO ASSOCIATED WITH THE SUREFORM, LOG_GRIP_RELEASE_TOOL_DETECTED AND ERROR_MIDDLEMAN_MANIP_MGR_MISUSE. ALL ERRORS WERE LOGGED AT THE SAME TIMESTAMP. THE USER MAY HAVE TURNED THE MANUAL RELEASE KNOB (MRK) ON THE SUREFORM INSTRUMENT, WHICH WOULD RESULT IN ALL ERRORS LOGGING AT THE SAME TIME. PER THE LOGS, THE LAST UNCLAMP WAS COMPLETED. NO IMAGE OR VIDEO CLIP FOR THE REPORTED EVENT WAS SUBMITTED FOR REVIEW. A MEDICAL REVIEW OF THE EVENT INFORMATION PROVIDED WAS PERFORMED BY AN ISI MEDICAL SAFETY OFFICER AND THE FOLLOWING INFORMATION WAS PROVIDED: BASED ON THE INFORMATION IN THE DESCRIPTION OF EVENTS, IT IS LIKELY THAT THE PATIENT EXPIRED DUE TO CARDIOPULMONARY FAILURE. BASED UPON THE DESCRIPTION OF EVENTS THE EXTENT AS TO THE CONTRIBUTION OF THE INTUITIVE SURGICAL SYSTEM TO THE PATIENTS MORTALITY IS NOT KNOWN. THIS COMPLAINT IS BEING REPORTED DUE TO THE FOLLOWING CONCLUSION: FOLLOWING A DA VINCI-ASSISTED SIGMOID COLECTOMY WITH COLO-ANAL ANASTOMOSIS PROCEDURE, THE PATIENT EXPIRED SECONDARY TO PULMONARY AND CARDIAC FAILURE. WHILE THERE WAS NO ALLEGATION OF A MALFUNCTION OF A DA VINCI SYSTEM, INSTRUMENT, OR ACCESSORY OCCURRED, THE EXTENT AS TO THE CONTRIBUTION OF THE DA VINCI SURGICAL SYSTEM TO THE PATIENTS MORTALITY IS NOT KNOWN.

THE SURGEONS IDENTIFIED A SMALL ENTRY SITE, AND THEY SAW EITHER THE GONADAL OR THE LUMBAR VESSEL BLEED AND CONTROLLED THE BLEED. THERE WAS A HEMATOMA, AND THE PATIENT WAS EXPERIENCING EXSANGUINATION. THE SURGEON WAS NOT SURE IF THE IVC WAS INJURED. SINCE THE ABDOMEN WAS NOT FILLING, SHE WAS NOT SURE IF THERE WAS A COMBINATION OF BLEEDING AND EMBOLI. THE PATIENT BECAME UNSTABLE AND SHE RECEIVED MULTIPLE CHEST COMPRESSIONS. HOWEVER, THE PATIENT ULTIMATELY EXPIRED ON THE TABLE. THERE IS NO VIDEO RECORDING OF THE PROCEDURE. THE DA VINCI SYSTEM WAS NOT DOCKED. SHE IS NOT SURE IF THE ISI TROCAR AND OBTURATOR WILL BE RETURNED TO ISI. AS PER THE SURGEON, THE CAUSE OF DEATH IS A HEMORRHAGIC SHOCK. HOWEVER, THERE IS AN AUTOPSY BEING PERFORMED FOR THE FINAL REPORT. AS OF THE DATE OF THIS REPORT, THE AUTOPSY REPORT IS UNAVAILABLE. ALTHOUGH AN ISI TROCAR WAS INVOLVED WITH THE VESSEL INJURY, THE SURGEON CONFIRMED NO MALFUNCTION OF THE DA VINCI SURGICAL SYSTEM, INSTRUMENT, OR ACCESSORY OCCURRED. ON A FOLLOW-UP WITH THE ISI EXECUTIVE SALES REPRESENTATIVE (ESR), SHE STATED THAT THIS SURGEON WAS NEW TO ROBOTICS AND SURGERIES IN GENERAL, AS SHE WAS OUT OF RESIDENCY SUMMER OF LAST YEAR (2021).

ON 03-MAY-2022, INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC. (ISI) BECAME AWARE OF A DISEASES OF THE ESOPHAGUS ARTICLE TITLED, END TO SIDE CIRCULAR STAPLED ANASTOMOSIS DURING ROBOTIC-ASSISTED IVOR LEWIS MINIMALLY INVASIVE ESOPHAGECTOMY (RAMIE) (CAPOVILLA, G., HADZIJUSUFOVIC, E., ET AL., 2022). WITHIN THE JOURNAL ARTICLE, POST-OPERATIVE COMPLICATIONS INVOLVING A DA VINCI-ASSISTED SURGICAL PROCEDURE WERE NOTED. A STUDY WAS CONDUCTED TO EVALUATE THE FEASIBILITY AND SAFETY OF SURGICAL TECHNIQUE FOR PERFORMING INTRATHORACIC ANASTOMOSIS DURING ROBOT-ASSISTED MINIMALLY INVASIVE ESOPHAGECTOMY (RAMIE). DATA WAS REVIEWED BETWEEN JANUARY 2016 AND APRIL 2021. IN THE STUDY PERIOD, 204 PATIENTS UNDERWENT RAMIE AND 122 PATIENTS WERE TREATED BY MINIMALLY INVASIVE ESOPHAGECTOMY (MIE) AS A CONTROL GROUP. IT WAS REPORTED THAT AFTER UNDERGOING DA VINCI-ASSISTED ESOPHAGECTOMY PROCEDURES, 21 ANASTOMOTIC LEAKS OCCURRED AND WERE PRESENT IN FOUR OF NINE PATIENTS WHO DIED POSTOPERATIVELY. ANASTOMOTIC STRICTURES WERE IDENTIFIED WITH 24 PATIENTS IN THE RAMIE GROUP. IT IS UNKNOWN WHETHER ADDITIONAL MEDICAL/SURGICAL INTERVENTION WAS ADMINISTERED DUE TO THE POST-OPERATIVE COMPLICATIONS. FURTHER DETAILS REGARDING THE CAUSES OF THE PATIENTS' POST-OPERATIVE DEATHS ARE UNKNOWN AT THIS TIME. FOR TWO PATIENTS WITHIN THE RAMIE GROUP, THE PROCEDURES WERE CONVERTED DURING THE THORACIC PHASE DUE TO INADVERTENT STAPLING OF THE CALIBRATION INTRALUMINAL BOUGIE DURING COMPLETION OF THE ANASTOMOSIS IN ONE CASE AND PRESENCE OF FIBROTIC ADHESIONS IN THE PLEURAL SPACE IN THE OTHER CASE. THERE WAS NO REPORT OR ALLEGATION FROM THE JOURNAL ARTICLE AUTHORS OF A SPECIFIC DEFICIENCY OF THE DA VINCI SYSTEM, INSTRUMENTATION OR ACCESSORIES ASSOCIATED WITH THE REPORTED INCIDENT. INTUITIVE SURGICAL INC. (ISI) MADE THE REQUIRED ATTEMPTS TO OBTAIN ADDITIONAL INFORMATION; HOWEVER, THERE WAS NO RESPONSE RECEIVED FROM THE ARTICLE CORRESPONDENT.

ON 04-MAY-2022, INTUITIVE BECAME AWARE OF AN EUROPEAN JOURNAL OF SURGICAL ONCOLOGY ARTICLE TITLED, A PROSPECTIVE LONGITUDINAL STUDY OF QUALITY OF LIFE IN ROBOTIC-ASSISTED SALVAGE SURGERY FOR OROPHARYNGEAL CANCER (DANDREA, G., BORDENAVE, L., ET AL., 2022). WITHIN THE JOURNAL ARTICLE, POST-OPERATIVE COMPLICATIONS AND DEATH INVOLVING A DA VINCI SURGICAL PROCEDURE WERE NOTED. A CLINICAL JOURNAL ARTICLE CITED THAT AN ANALYSIS OF DATA WAS CONDUCTED IN AN OBSERVATIONAL COHORT STUDY OF QUALITY OF LIFE IN ROBOTIC-ASSISTED SALVAGE SURGERY FOR OROPHARYNGEAL CANCER (OPC). DURING THE STUDY PERIOD, 53 PATIENTS UNDERWENT ROBOTIC-ASSISTED SURGERY. THE MEAN AGE WAS 64.3 YEARS. BETWEEN SEPTEMBER 2015 AND JANUARY 2021, ALL PATIENTS WHO UNDERWENT ROBOTIC SURGERY USING THE DA VINCI XI SURGICAL SYSTEM FOR A HISTOLOGICALLY PROVEN OPC ARISING IN A PREVIOUSLY IRRADIATED SITE WERE INCLUDED IN THE STUDY. THE PROCEDURES WERE EITHER EXCLUSIVELY TRANSORAL ROBOTIC SURGERY, OR COMBINED CERVICAL TRANSORAL ROBOTIC SURGERY, ACCORDING TO THE SIZE OF THE TUMOR, THE DEPTH OF INFILTRATION, AND THE ANATOMICAL CONSTRAINTS (NOTABLY DEEP VASCULAR SAFETY). MEDIAN FOLLOW-UP WAS 674 DAYS. COMPLICATIONS WERE RATED ACCORDING TO THE CLAVIEN-DINDO CLASSIFICATION. THE MAIN POSTOPERATIVE COMPLICATIONS WERE PULMONARY INFECTIONS, FOR 17 PATIENTS (32%). A POSTOPERATIVE HEMORRHAGE OF THE LOCAL SITE OCCURRED IN TWO PATIENTS (3.8%) AND A NECK HEMATOMA IN FOUR PATIENTS (7.6%). TWO PATIENTS REQUIRED A POSTOPERATIVE TRACHEOSTOMY FOR ACUTE RESPIRATORY DISTRESS. TWO PATIENTS DIED OF POSTOPERATIVE MULTI ORGAN FAILURE FOLLOWING SEVERE PULMONARY INFECTIONS DESPITE A PROPHYLACTIC TRACHEOSTOMY. PER THE AUTHORS, SALVAGE ROBOTIC-ASSISTED SURGERY FOR OPC MAY BE PREFERRED FOR PATIENTS WITH TUMORS AMENABLE TO EITHER A TRANSORAL OR A COMBINED CERVICAL TRANSORAL ROBOTIC APPROACHES. INTUITIVE SURGICAL INC. (ISI) MADE THE REQUIRED ATTEMPTS TO OBTAIN ADDITIONAL INFORMATION; HOWEVER, THERE WAS NO RESPONSE RECEIVED FROM THE PHYSICIAN.

INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC. (ISI) BECAME AWARE OF A SURGERY TODAY ARTICLE TITLED, REDUCED-PORT ROBOTIC PANCREATICODUODENECTOMY VERSUS OPEN PANCREATICODUODENECTOMY: A SINGLE-SURGEON EXPERIENCE (CHIANG, C, CHIANG, C., ET AL., 2022). WITHIN THE JOURNAL ARTICLE, POST-OPERATIVE DEATHS INVOLVING DA VINCI-ASSISTED SURGICAL PROCEDURES WERE NOTED: THERE WAS ONE DEATH WITHIN 30 DAYS AND 4 DEATHS WITHIN 90 DAYS IN THE REDUCED-PORT ROBOTIC PANCREATICODUODENECTOMY (RPD) GROUP. THE OBJECTIVE OF THE STUDY WAS TO COMPARE THE SAFETY AND EFFICACY OF REDUCED-PORT RPD VERSUS OPEN PANCREATICODUODENECTOMY (OPD). THIS PROSPECTIVE COHORT STUDY COMPRISED OF ADULT PATIENTS WHO UNDERWENT REDUCED-PORT RPD (SINGLE-PORT OR SINGLE-SITE PLUS ONE PORT) OR OPD FOR MALIGNANT TUMORS OF THE PANCREAS AND PERIAMPULLARY REGION FROM JULY 2015 TO OCTOBER 2020 AT A SINGLE MEDICAL CENTER. INCLUDED IN THE STUDY WERE 45 ADULT PATIENTS 18 YEARS AND OLDER WHO UNDERWENT A REDUCED-PORT RPD AND 13 PATIENTS WHO UNDERWENT OPD. THE SURGICAL TECHNIQUE DESCRIBED IN THE OBSERVATIONAL STUDY INCLUDED TWO TYPES OF REDUCED-PORT RPD: SINGLE-PORT AND SINGLE-SITE PLUS ONE PORT RPD. DATA WAS COLLECTED ON THE PATIENT DEMOGRAPHICS, PERIOPERATIVE RESULTS, ONCOLOGIC OUTCOMES, AND ONE YEAR SURVIVAL. THE 30-DAY AND 90-DAY MORTALITY BETWEEN REDUCED-PORT RPD AND OPD WAS COMPARED TO EVALUATE THE SAFETY PROFILE OF THE PROCEDURES. TO DETERMINE THE FACTORS ASSOCIATED WITH 90-DAY MORTALITY, MULTIVARIATE LOGISTIC REGRESSION BASED ON PREDEFINED CLINICAL VARIABLES WERE PERFORMED. A SUBGROUP ANALYSIS TO COMPARE SINGLE-PORT RPD AND SINGLE-SITE PLUS ONE PORT RPD WAS ALSO EVALUATED. THE STUDY RECORDED 5 POST-OPERATIVE DEATHS THAT OCCURRED AFTER A DA VINCI SYSTEM RPD PROCEDURE. WITHIN THE ARTICLE, IT IS NOTED THAT IN THE STUDY, THESE DEATHS WERE ASSOCIATED WITH INFECTIONS THAT DEVELOPED MORE THAN 20 DAYS AFTER SURGERY. FURTHERMORE, IT IS NOTED: "OUR FINDINGS WERE CONSISTENT WITH PREVIOUS STUDIES, WHICH SHOWED THAT THE DEVELOPMENT OF POSTOPERATIVE COMPLICATIONS WAS A RISK FACTOR FOR A REDUCTION IN THE OVERALL SURVIVAL IN PATIENTS WHO UNDERGO CURATIVE SURGERY FOR PANCREATIC CANCER [12]. TAKEN TOGETHER, OUR RESULTS SUGGEST THAT WHILE THE REDUCED-PORT RPD PROCEDURE IS SAFE, POSTOPERATIVE MANAGEMENT OF PATIENTS WHO UNDERGO PD REMAINS IMPORTANT [14]." ISI HAS REACHED OUT TO THE AUTHOR TO OBTAIN ADDITIONAL INFORMATION BUT HAS NOT YET RECEIVED A RESPONSE.

INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC. (ISI) BECAME AWARE OF A CENTRAL EUROPEAN JOURNAL OF UROLOGY ARTICLE TITLED, LEARNING CURVE IN ROBOT-ASSISTED PARTIAL NEPHRECTOMY: COMPARISON BETWEEN AN EXPERT SURGEON AND A TEAM IN TRAINING IN SINGLE-CENTER EXPERIENCES (FIORELLO, N., DI BENEDETTO, A., ET AL., 2022). WITHIN THE JOURNAL ARTICLE, INTRAOPERATIVE COMPLICATIONS LEADING TO PATIENT DEATH INVOLVING A DA VINCI SURGICAL PROCEDURE WERE NOTED. FROM JANUARY 2014 TO DECEMBER 2020, 333 PATIENTS WERE RETROSPECTIVELY ENROLLED, UNDERGOING ROBOT-ASSISTED PARTIAL NEPHRECTOMY (RAPN). THE FOLLOWING ELEMENTS WERE EVALUATED FOR EACH PATIENT: SURGICAL COMPLEXITY, DURATION, PERIOPERATIVE COMPLICATIONS, AND CLAMPING. PER THE ARTICLE, "COMPARISONS WERE MADE BETWEEN AN EXPERIENCED SURGEON AND 3 UROLOGISTS WITH INITIAL EXPERIENCE IN ROBOTIC SURGERY." THE FOLLOWING WAS NOTED IN THE ARTICLE: INTRAOPERATIVE COMPLICATIONS OCCURRED IN 3 PATIENTS: ONE CASE FOR THE EXPERIENCED SURGEON WITH PERFORATION OF THE ILEUM, WHICH REQUIRED OPEN CONVERSION, WITH ADMISSION TO INTENSIVE CARE AND SUBSEQUENT DEATH OF THE PATIENT., ONE CASE OF SPLENIC INJURY FOR SURGEON 2, RESOLVED WITH THE APPLICATION OF TACHOSIL AND SUBSEQUENT REGULAR COURSE UNTIL DISCHARGE, AND ONE CASE OF COLON INJURY FOR SURGEON 3, RESOLVED WITH INTRACORPOREAL SUTURE AND SUBSEQUENT REGULAR COURSE UNTIL DISCHARGE. ALSO, PER THE ARTICLE, POST-OPERATIVE COMPLICATIONS OCCURRED IN 14 PATIENTS. TWELVE POST-OPERATIVE COMPLICATIONS WERE ASSOCIATED WITH THE EXPERIENCED SURGEON OF WHICH EIGHT PATIENTS WERE CLAVIEN 1 AND TWO PATIENTS CLAVIEN 3 (ONE PATIENT HAD A PULMONARY EMBOLISM, TREATED WITH MEDICAL THERAPY AND ADMISSION TO INTENSIVE CARE AND ANOTHER PATIENT HAD EXTRAVASATION OF URINE, TREATED WITH DJ URETERAL CATHETER PLACEMENT). ONE PATIENT FOR SURGEON 1 EXPERIENCED ANEMIA WHICH WAS TREATED WITH BLOOD TRANSFUSION [CLAVIEN 2]) AND ONE PATIENT FOR SURGEON 3 EXPERIENCED AN UNSPECIFIED COMPLICATION (CLAVIEN 1). ON 30-MAY-2022, INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC (ISI) RECEIVED ADDITIONAL INFORMATION ABOUT THE CLINICAL ARTICLE: THERE WAS NO REPORT OR ALLEGATION FROM THE CUSTOMER OF A MALFUNCTION OF A DA VINCI SYSTEM, INSTRUMENT, OR ACCESSORY ASSOCIATED WITH THE REPORTED INCIDENT. THE DATE OF THE PROCEDURE OCCURRED ON 2020 (SPECIFIC DATE NOT PROVIDED). IN THE CASE OF THE PATIENT WITH THE PERFORATED ILEUM, THE ILEUM WAS PUNCTURED DURING THE PLACEMENT OF THE FIRST TROCAR RESULTING IN THE CONVERSION TO OPEN SURGERY. THE SURGEON BELIEVED THAT THE CAUSE OF THE INTRA-OPERATIVE COMPLICATION WAS THE PRESENCE OF "TOO MANY ABDOMINAL ADHESIONS." THE PATIENT REPORTED IN THE CLINICAL ARTICLE WAS AN ELDERLY PATIENT WITH A HISTORY OF MULTIPLE SURGICAL PROCEDURES AND MANY ADHESIONS. THE PATIENT'S CAUSE OF DEATH AND AUTOPSY REPORT WAS NOT PROVIDED. THE PATIENT'S DEMOGRAPHIC INFORMATION WAS NOT PROVIDED. NO PRODUCTS ARE AVAILABLE FOR ISI TO EVALUATE AS THERE WAS NO MALFUNCTION.

BASED ON THE CURRENT INFORMATION PROVIDED, THE CAUSE OF THE PATIENTS OPERATIVE COMPLICATIONS AND DEATH ARE UNKNOWN. ISI HAS ATTEMPTED TO CONTACT THE SITE TO GATHER ADDITIONAL INFORMATION REGARDING THE PATIENT/INCIDENT. HOWEVER, AS OF THE DATE OF THIS REPORT, NO NEW INFORMATION HAS BEEN OBTAINED. IF ADDITIONAL INFORMATION IS RECEIVED, A FOLLOW-UP MDR WILL BE SUBMITTED. A REVIEW OF THE SYSTEM LOGS FOR THE PROCEDURE DATE OF (B)(6) 2022 HAS BEEN PERFORMED BY AN ISI TECHNICAL SUPPORT ENGINEER (TSE) AND THE FOLLOWING WAS OBSERVED: NO PROCEDURES EXCEPT FOR ONE TRAINING EVENT HAVE BEEN PERFORMED ON THIS SYSTEM AFTER THIS REPORTED COMPLAINT AND NO SERVICE REQUESTS HAVE BEEN CREATED. NO RELEVANT ERRORS WERE OBSERVED DURING THIS PROCEDURE. AS A RESULT OF THIS REVIEW, THE TSE DID NOT RECOMMEND AN ISI FIELD SERVICE ENGINEER (FSE) VISIT THE SITE TO PERFORM SYSTEM VERIFICATION TESTING. NO IMAGE OR VIDEO CLIP FOR THE REPORTED EVENT WAS SUBMITTED FOR REVIEW. A REVIEW OF THE EVENT WAS CONDUCTED BY AN ISI MEDICAL SAFETY OFFICER (MSO) AND THE FOLLOWING ADDITIONAL INFORMATION WAS PROVIDED: BASED UPON THE INFORMATION IN THE DESCRIPTION OF EVENTS, THE ISI MSO IS UNABLE TO MAKE ANY DETERMINATION ABOUT THE CAUSE OF THE PATIENTS MORTALITY. THERE IS NO INFORMATION TO DETERMINE THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE PORTAL VEIN INJURY AND THE MORTALITY. HOWEVER, THE PORTAL VEIN INJURY DID OCCUR DURING THE PORTION OF THE PROCEDURE USING THE DA VINCI SYSTEM AND INSTRUMENTATION. THE CIRCUMSTANCES SURROUNDING THE EVENT ARE NOT PROVIDED IN THE DESCRIPTION OF EVENT TO MAKE ANY DETERMINATION AS TO THE POSSIBLE ROOT CAUSE OF THE PORTAL VEIN INJURY. THIS COMPLAINT IS BEING REPORTED DUE TO THE FOLLOWING CONCLUSION: DURING A DA VINCI-ASSISTED PANCREATICODUODENECTOMY PROCEDURE, THERE WAS BLEEDING DUE TO DETACHMENT OF THE PORTAL VEIN DURING DISSECTION AROUND THE PORTAL VEIN. BLEEDING COULD NOT BE CONTROLLED ROBOTICALLY AND AS A RESULT, THE PROCEDURE WAS CONVERTED TO OPEN SURGERY. THE PATIENT LOST 2500 ML OF BLOOD AND RECEIVED A BLOOD TRANSFUSION. AFTER STOPPING THE BLEEDING VIA OPEN SURGERY, THE DA VINCI ROBOT WAS RE-DOCKED. THE SUTURE BROKE SEVERAL TIMES WHICH PREVENTED THE SURGEON FROM CONTINUING THE DA VINCI-ASSISTED SURGICAL PROCEDURE. THE SURGERY WAS THEN CONVERTED TO OPEN SURGERY AGAIN TO AVOID PROLONGATION OF PROCEDURE. THE PROCEDURE WAS COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY VIA OPEN SURGERY. POST-OPERATIVELY, UNSPECIFIED COMPLICATIONS OCCURRED ON POD #4 AND THE PATIENT DIED ON POD #4. THE CAUSES OF THE PATIENT'S OPERATIVE COMPLICATIONS AND SUBSEQUENT DEATH ARE UNKNOWN. BLANK MDR FIELDS FOLLOW-UP WITH THE SURGEON WAS ATTEMPTED, BUT THE MISSING PATIENT INFORMATION IN SECTIONS WERE EITHER UNKNOWN, UNAVAILABLE, NOT PROVIDED, OR NOT APPLICABLE. IT WAS REPORTED THAT DURING A DA VINCI-ASSISTED PANCREATICODUODENECTOMY PROCEDURE, THERE WAS BLEEDING DUE TO DETACHMENT OF THE PORTAL VEIN DURING DISSECTION AROUND THE VESSEL. BLEEDING COULD NOT BE CONTROLLED ROBOTICALLY, AND THE PROCEDURE WAS CONVERTED TO OPEN. POST-OPERATIVELY, UNSPECIFIED COMPLICATIONS OCCURRED, AND THE PATIENT DIED ON POST-OPERATIVE DAY (POD) #4. INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC. (ISI) FOLLOWED UP WITH THE ISI CLINICAL SALES REPRESENTATIVE (CSR) WHO WAS PRESENT DURING THE PROCEDURE AND OBTAINED THE FOLLOWING INFORMATION: THE AMOUNT OF BLEEDING WHEN THE PATIENT SIDE CART (PSC) WAS FIRST UNDOCKED WAS MORE THAN 2500 ML AND THE PATIENT WAS TRANSFUSED WITH BLOOD. THE PROCEDURE WAS CONVERTED TO OPEN TO CONTROL THE BLEEDING. AFTER STOPPING THE BLEEDING VIA OPEN SURGERY, THE DA VINCI ROBOT WAS RE-DOCKED, AND THE SURGEON CONTINUED THE PROCEDURE ROBOTICALLY AGAIN AS HE BELIEVED THAT THE DA VINCI SYSTEM ENABLED HIM TO PERFORM THE HIGHEST LEVEL OF PROCEDURE. AFTER RE-DOCKING AND CONTINUING THE PROCEDURE ROBOTICALLY, THE 5-0 SUTURE (A NON-ISI PRODUCT) BROKE SEVERAL TIMES WHICH PREVENTED THE SURGEON FROM CONTINUING THE DA VINCI PROCEDURE. THE SURGEON CONVERTED THE PROCEDURE TO OPEN SURGERY AGAIN TO AVOID A PROCEDURE PROLONGATION. THE SURGERY WAS COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY VIA OPEN SURGERY. THE NURSE WHO ASSISTED IN THE PROCEDURE CONFIRMED THAT THE ONSET OF THE POST-OPERATIVE COMPLICATION AND DATE OF DEATH WAS CONFIRMED TO BE (B)(6) 2022. ISI MADE MULTIPLE FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPTS TO OBTAIN ADDITIONAL INFORMATION FROM THE SURGEON. HOWEVER, NO FURTHER DETAILS HAVE BEEN RECEIVED AS OF THE DATE OF THIS REPORT.

IT WAS INITIALLY REPORTED THAT PRIOR TO THE START OF A DA VINCI-ASSISTED INCARCERATED HERNIA REPAIR AND AFTER ANESTHESIA WAS ADMINISTERED, THE PATIENT CODED AS THE SURGICAL STAFF STARTED THE INSUFFLATION. IT IS UNKNOWN HOW MANY PORTS WERE PLACED PRIOR TO WHEN THE EVENT OCCURRED. IN ADDITION, IT IS UNKNOWN HOW THE PORT THAT WAS USED FOR INSUFFLATION WAS PLACED. RESUSCITATIVE EFFORTS WERE ADMINISTERED; HOWEVER, THE PATIENT SUBSEQUENTLY EXPIRED. ON 26-MAY-2022, INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC (ISI) OBTAINED ADDITIONAL INFORMATION ABOUT THE COMPLAINT. THE SURGEON STATED THAT THE PATIENT WAS A 76-YEAR-OLD  AFRICAN AMERICAN FEMALE SCHEDULED FOR A DA VINCI-ASSISTED INCARCERATED HERNIA REPAIR. ONCE INSUFFLATION WAS INITIATED, HER BLOOD PRESSURE DROPPED AND WENT INTO PULSELESS ELECTRICAL ACTIVITY (PEA) AND INTERVENTIONS WERE INITIATED AND THE PATIENT EXPIRED. THE PATIENT HAS A HISTORY OF STROKE 2 YEARS AGO. THE SURGEON STATED SHE HAS NO OTHER INFORMATION TO PROVIDE.
BASED ON THE CURRENT INFORMATION PROVIDED, THE CAUSE OF THE PATIENTS OPERATIVE COMPLICATION LEADING TO HER DEMISE CANNOT BE DETERMINED. THERE IS NO ALLEGATION THAT A MALFUNCTION OF A DA VINCI SYSTEM, INSTRUMENT, OR ACCESSORY OCCURRED. A FOLLOW-UP MDR WILL BE SUBMITTED IF ADDITIONAL INFORMATION IS OBTAINED. A REVIEW OF THE SYSTEM AND INSTRUMENT LOGS HAS BEEN PERFORMED BY AN ISI TECHNICAL SUPPORT ENGINEER (FSE) FOR PROCEDURES PERFORMED ON DA VINCI SYSTEM SK1903 ON (B)(6) 2022 . THERE WERE NO OBSERVED EVENTS IN THE SYSTEM LOGS THAT WOULD SUGGEST A PRODUCT ISSUE AND LOGGED EVENTS ARE IN LINE WITH NORMAL SYSTEM FUNCTIONALITY. NO IMAGE OR VIDEO CLIPS FOR THE REPORTED EVENT WERE SUBMITTED BY THE CUSTOMER FOR REVIEW. THE EVENT INFORMATION PROVIDED AS BEEN REVIEWED BY AN ISI MEDICAL SAFETY OFFICER (MSO) AND THE FOLLOWING WAS NOTED: "THERE IS INSUFFICIENT INFORMATION TO DETERMINE THE CAUSE OF THE PATIENTS PULSELESS ELECTRICAL ACTIVITY (PEA) DURING INSUFFLATION DURING AN ATTEMPTED DA VINCI-ASSISTED INCARCERATED HERNIA REPAIR." THIS COMPLAINT IS BEING REPORTED DUE TO THE FOLLOWING CONCLUSION: PRIOR TO THE START OF A DA VINCI-ASSISTED INCARCERATED HERNIA REPAIR, AFTER ANESTHESIA WAS ADMINISTERED TO THE PATIENT AND INSUFFLATION WAS INITIATED, THE PATIENT CODED. THE PATIENT WAS UNABLE TO BE REVIVED AND THE PATIENT SUBSEQUENTLY EXPIRED. AT THIS TIME, THE ROOT CAUSE OF THE PATIENTS OPERATIVE COMPLICATION LEADING TO HER DEATH IS UNKNOWN. THERE IS NO ALLEGATION THAT A MALFUNCTION OF A DA VINCI SYSTEM, INSTRUMENT OR ACCESSORY OCCURRED.

BRAND_NAME: DA VINCI X ROBOTIC SURGERY UNIT
FOI_TEXT: THE PATIENT HAD A MILD HERNIA THAT NEEDED REPAIR. IT WAS DONE ROBOTICALLY USING THE DAVINCI SYSTEM. THE PATIENT HAD A PUNCTURED ARTERY AND A LACERATED BOWEL FROM SURGICAL ERRORS DURING THE PROCEDURE. THERE WAS EXTENSIVE INTERNAL BLEEDING. THE PATIENT BECAME SEPTIC AND WITHIN THREE DAYS WAS DEAD. THE PATIENT WAS HEALTHY WHEN HE WALKED IN EVEN THOUGH HE WAS AN OLDER PATIENT. THE PATIENT WENT TO THE HOSPITAL WITH UPPER ABDOMINAL PAIN. IT WAS DETERMINED HE HAD A HERNIA AND HE WAS SENT TO A DOCTOR TO HAVE IT REPAIRED. FDA SAFETY REPORT ID # (B)(4).

IT WAS REPORTED THAT DURING A DA VINCI-ASSISTED LIVER RESECTION PROCEDURE, THE INFERIOR VENA CAVA (IVC) WAS DAMAGED AND THE PATIENT BLED OUT AND EXPIRED IN THE OPERATING ROOM. THE SPECIFIC TYPE OF LIVER RESECTION PERFORMED WAS NOT DISCLOSED. ACCORDING TO THE INITIAL REPORTER, AN INTUITIVE SURGICAL INC. (ISI) CLINICAL SALES REPRESENTATIVE (CSR), WHO WAS PRESENT IN THE OPERATING ROOM AT THE TIME OF THE EVENT, THE SURGEON REPORTEDLY "NICKED" THE IVC WITH AN ENERGIZED PERMANENT CAUTERY HOOK (PCH) INSTRUMENT. THIS CAUSED THE IVC TO START BLEEDING. IT IS UNKNOWN WHAT SURGICAL TASK THE SURGEON WAS PERFORMING WHEN THE INTRA-OPERATIVE COMPLICATION OCCURRED. THE CSR SAID HE DID NOT OBSERVE THE SURGEON CLAMP ONTO THE IVC, RATHER THE PROCEDURE WAS IMMEDIATELY CONVERTED TO OPEN SURGERY. THE CSR OBSERVED THE OR STAFF UNDOCK THE DA VINCI SYSTEM; MEANWHILE, THE PATIENT CODED. THE OR STAFF GAVE THE PATIENT CHEST COMPRESSIONS AND THE PATIENT'S STERNUM WAS OPENED. THE OPERATING ROOM STAFF CALLED VASCULAR SURGEONS AND TRANSPLANT SURGEONS INTO THE ROOM. THE CSR LEFT THE OPERATING ROOM AT THIS POINT AND HAD NO FURTHER INFORMATION TO PROVIDE. THE CSR SAID THE CUSTOMER HAS NOT MADE ANY ALLEGATIONS AGAINST A DA VINCI PRODUCT. ISI HAS ATTEMPTED TO OBTAIN ADDITIONAL INFORMATION FROM THE SURGEON OF THIS PROCEDURE. HOWEVER, AS OF THE DATE OF THIS REPORT, NO FURTHER DETAILS HAVE BEEN RECEIVED.
BASED ON THE INFORMATION PROVIDED, THE CAUSE OF THE REPORTED INTRA-COMPLICATION LEADING TO THE PATIENT'S DEMISE CANNOT BE DETERMINED. THERE IS NO ALLEGATION THAT A MALFUNCTION OF A DA VINCI SYSTEM, INSTRUMENT, OR ACCESSORY OCCURRED. HOWEVER, THE CSR REPORTED THAT A PCH INSTRUMENT WAS INVOLVED WITH THE INJURY TO THE IVC; THEREFORE, ISI HAS REQUESTED FOR RETURN OF THIS INSTRUMENT TO ISI FOR FAILURE ANALYSIS EVALUATION. THE PCH INSTRUMENT HAS NOT BEEN RETURNED AS OF THE TIME OF THIS REPORT. A FOLLOW-UP MDR WILL BE SUBMITTED IF ADDITIONAL INFORMATION IS OBTAINED. A REVIEW OF THE SYSTEM LOGS FOR THE PROCEDURE DATE OF (B)(6) 2022 HAS BEEN PERFORMED BY AN ISI TECHNICAL SUPPORT ENGINEER (TSE) AND THE FOLLOWING WAS OBSERVED: ONE PROCEDURE HAS BEEN PERFORMED ON THIS SYSTEM AFTER THIS REPORTED COMPLAINT. NO RELEVANT ERRORS WERE OBSERVED DURING THIS PROCEDURE. AS A RESULT OF THIS REVIEW, THE TSE DID NOT RECOMMEND A FIELD SERVICE ENGINEER (FSE) VISIT THE SITE TO PERFORM SYSTEM VERIFICATION TESTING. A REVIEW OF THE INSTRUMENT USED DURING THIS PROCEDURE WAS PERFORMED. ALL MULTI-USE INSTRUMENTS USED IN THE CASE HAVE BEEN USED IN SUBSEQUENT PROCEDURES WITH THE EXCEPTION OF THE SINGLE-USE INSTRUMENTS AND THE FOLLOWING INSTRUMENT: PERMANENT CAUTERY HOOK INSTRUMENT (PART #470183-14; LOT #K14220110-0039). NO CUSTOMER COMPLAINTS HAVE BEEN MADE AGAINST THIS INSTRUMENT OR ANY OTHER INSTRUMENTS USED DURING THIS PROCEDURE. A MEDICAL REVIEW OF THE EVENT INFORMATION WAS PERFORMED BY AN ISI MEDICAL SAFETY OFFICER AND THE FOLLOWING INFORMATION WAS PROVIDED: "BASED UPON THE INFORMATION IN THE DESCRIPTION OF EVENTS, IT IS NOT CLEAR AS TO WHY THE IVC WAS 'NICKED' BY THE PERMANENT CAUTERY HOOK. ADDITIONAL INVESTIGATION IS REQUIRED TO BETTER UNDERSTAND THIS EVENT." THIS COMPLAINT IS BEING REPORTED DUE TO THE FOLLOWING CONCLUSION: DURING A DA VINCI-ASSISTED LIVER RESECTION PROCEDURE, THE IVC WAS DAMAGED AND THE PATIENT BLED OUT IN THE OPERATING ROOM. ACCORDING TO THE INITIAL REPORTER, THE VESSEL WAS "NICKED" BY AN ENERGIZED PCH INSTRUMENT. AT THIS TIME, THE ROOT CAUSE OF THE COMPLICATION LEADING TO THE PATIENT'S DEMISE IS UNKNOWN. THERE IS NO ALLEGATION THAT A MALFUNCTION OF A DA VINCI SYSTEM, INSTRUMENT, OR ACCESSORY OCCURRED.
ON 19-JUL-2022, INTUITIVE SURGICAL INC. (ISI) CONTACTED THE SURGEON OF THIS PROCEDURE AND ADDITIONAL INFORMATION WAS OBTAINED ABOUT THE COMPLAINT: THE SURGEON CLARIFIED THAT THE EVENT SHOULD BE THE PATIENT WAS UNABLE TO BE RESUSCITATED FOLLOWING REPAIR OF THE IVC. IT WAS REPORTED THAT THE PATIENT EXPIRED THREE DAYS AFTER UNDERGOING A DA-VINCI ASSISTED CHOLECYSTECTOMY SURGICAL PROCEDURE. ON (B)(6) 2020, INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC. (ISI) FOLLOWED-UP WITH THE INITIAL REPORTER AND OBTAINED THE FOLLOWING ADDITIONAL INFORMATION: THERE WAS NO MALFUNCTION OF AN ISI SYSTEM, INSTRUMENT, OR ACCESSORY. ALL DEVICES WERE INSPECTED PRIOR TO USE AND NO DAMAGE WAS IDENTIFIED. ADDITIONALLY, THERE WERE NO INTRAOPERATIVE COMPLICATIONS. THE PROCEDURE WAS SUCCESSFULLY COMPLETED WITH THE DA VINCI SURGICAL SYSTEM. APPROXIMATELY TEN HOURS FOLLOWING PROCEDURE COMPLETION, THE PATIENT EXPERIENCED ACUTE HEART FAILURE AND WAS TRANSPORTED TO THE INTENSIVE CARE DEPARTMENT ON THE SAME EVENING. THE PATIENT REMAINED IN THE INTENSIVE CARE UNIT UNTIL (B)(6) 2020 WHEN THE ANESTHESIA DOCTORS AND THE PATIENTS FAMILY DECIDED TO HALT TREATMENT LEADING TO THE PATIENTS DEATH. IT WAS REPORTED THAT THE ANESTHESIA TEAM AT THE SITE FOUND SOME INDICATORS THAT THE PATIENT HAD TAKOTSUBO BEFORE THE PROCEDURE BUT DID NOT OPT TO HALT THE PROCEDURE. THE ANESTHESIA TEAM REPORTED THAT THE CAUSE OF DEATH WAS TAKOTSUBO CARDIOMYOPATHY. IT WAS NOTED THAT COMPUTED TOMOGRAPHY (CT) SCANS WERE TAKEN OF THE PATIENT AT APPROXIMATELY 48 HOURS AND 72 HOURS FOLLOWING PROCEDURE COMPLETION AND EVERYTHING WAS REPORTED TO LOOK NORMAL; THERE WAS NO DAMAGE TO THE COLON, NO LEAKS, AND NO FLUID IN THE STOMACH.
A REVIEW OF THE SYSTEM AND INSTRUMENT LOGS HAS BEEN PERFORMED FOR EACH OF THE THREE PROCEDURES PERFORMED WITH THE SYSTEM ON THE REPORTED DATE OF THE PROCEDURE. THERE WERE NO OBSERVED EVENTS IN THE SYSTEM LOGS THAT WOULD SUGGEST A PRODUCT ISSUE, AND LOGGED EVENTS ARE IN LINE WITH NORMAL SYSTEM FUNCTIONALITY. THE LOG REVIEW SUPPORTS THE CUSTOMER CLAIM THAT THERE WAS NO SYSTEM ISSUE DURING THE CASE. ADDITIONALLY, EACH ENDOSCOPE AND INSTRUMENT INSTALLED IN EACH OF THE PROCEDURES WAS USED IN SUBSEQUENT PROCEDURES. NO IMAGE OR VIDEO CLIP FOR THE REPORTED EVENT WAS SUBMITTED FOR REVIEW. AN ISI MEDICAL SAFETY OFFICER REVIEWED THE CASE INFORMATION AND PROVIDED THE FOLLOWING: BASED UPON THE INFORMATION PROVIDED IN THE DESCRIPTION OF EVENTS, THE PATIENT EXPIRED 3 DAYS AFTER A DA VINCI-ASSISTED CHOLECYSTECTOMY DUE TO ACUTE HEART FAILURE. IMAGING STUDIES PERFORMED AFTER THE PROCEDURE FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE ANY EVIDENCE OF AN INTRA-ABDOMINAL COMPLICATION THAT WOULD CAUSE OR CONTRIBUTE TO THE PATIENTS ACUTE HEART FAILURE. THE CT SCAN WAS REPORTED AS NORMAL WITH NO REPORTED INJURY TO THE COLON OR STOMACH. THERE WERE NO REPORTED MALFUNCTIONS OR COMPLICATIONS DURING THE PROCEDURE. THERE IS NO ALLEGATION THAT THE DEATH WAS CAUSED OR CONTRIBUTED BY THE DA VINCI SYSTEM, INSTRUMENTATION, AND ACCESSORIES. BOTH THE ATTENDING SURGEON OF RECORD AND THE ANESTHESIA TEAM INDICATED THAT PATIENTS CAUSE OF ACUTE HEART FAILURE AND DEATH WERE DUE TO A PRE-EXISTING MEDICAL CONDITION. BASED UPON THE INFORMATION, IT IS VERY UNLIKELY THAT THE DA VINCI SYSTEM, INSTRUMENTATION, AND ACCESSORIES CAUSED OR CONTRIBUTED TO THE PATIENTS DEATH. BASED ON THE CURRENT INFORMATION PROVIDED, THE CAUSE OF THE REPORTED ACUTE HEART FAILURE AND SUBSEQUENT PATIENT EXPIRATION IS SUSPECTED TO BE THE PATIENTS PRE-EXISTING CONDITION, TAKOTSUBO CARDIOMYOPATHY. THERE WAS NO ALLEGATION THAT A MALFUNCTION OF A DA VINCI SYSTEM, INSTRUMENT, OR ACCESSORY OCCURRED. IF ADDITIONAL INFORMATION IS RECEIVED, A FOLLOW-UP MDR WILL BE SUBMITTED. THIS COMPLAINT IS BEING REPORTED DUE TO THE FOLLOWING CONCLUSION: APPROXIMATELY TEN HOURS FOLLOWING A DA VINCI-ASSISTED CHOLECYSTECTOMY SURGICAL PROCEDURE, THE PATIENT EXPERIENCED ACUTE HEART FAILURE REQUIRING ADMITTANCE TO INTENSIVE CARE WHERE SHE SUBSEQUENTLY EXPIRED FOLLOWING A DECISION TO TERMINATE LIFE SUSTAINING CARE. WHILE THE SITE ATTRIBUTED THE PATIENTS ACUTE HEART FAILURE TO THE PATIENTS PRE-EXISTING CONDITION, TAKOTSUBO CARDIOMYOPATHY, IT IS UNKNOWN IF THE REPORTED PATIENT COMPLICATION AND SUBSEQUENT EXPIRATION ARE RELATED EITHER DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY TO THE DA VINCI-ASSISTED PROCEDURE. IT WAS REPORTED THAT AFTER A SUCCESSFUL A DA VINCI-ASSISTED RADICAL HYSTERECTOMY PROCEDURE IN AN OBESE PATIENT, THE PATIENT COMPLAINED OF ABDOMINAL PAIN A FEW DAYS POST-PROCEDURE. AN INVESTIGATION PERFORMED BY GENERAL SURGEONS FOUND AN UNSPECIFIED INJURY ON THE BOWEL. A LAPAROSCOPIC PROCEDURE WAS PERFORMED ON THE PATIENT BY A GENERAL SURGEON ON AN UNSPECIFIED DATE. A FEW DAYS LATER, THE PATIENT'S CONDITION DETERIORATED. SHE UNDERWENT A SECOND PROCEDURE WHEREBY THE COLORECTAL SURGEONS DISCOVERED HER ABDOMEN WAS INFECTED DUE TO AN "INSUFFICIENT ANASTOMOSIS." THE PATIENT PASSED AWAY ON AN UNSPECIFIED DATE. INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC (ISI) FOLLOWED UP WITH THE CLINICAL SALES REPRESENTATIVE (CSR), WHO FOLLOWED UP WITH THE SURGEON AND NURSES, AND OBTAINED THE FOLLOWING INFORMATION: DURING THE PRIMARY PROCEDURE, THERE WERE ADHESIONS SEEN. IT IS UNKNOWN IF THE ADHESIONS WERE DENSE/EXTENSIVE. THERE WERE NO INTRA-OPERATIVE COMPLICATIONS DURING THE PRIMARY PROCEDURE AND NO MALFUNCTION OF AN ISI PRODUCT OCCURRED. THERE WAS NO INCIDENT OF ARCING/BURNS/THERMAL INJURY. THERE WERE NO RESTRICTIONS OR LIMITED ABDOMINAL SPACE TO MOVE INSTRUMENTS. ADDITIONALLY, THERE WAS NO EVIDENCE OF DIVERTICULITIS. THE PATIENT COMPLAINED OF ABDOMINAL PAIN ABOUT 5-7 DAYS AFTER THE PRIMARY PROCEDURE. A CT SCAN, AS WELL AS LABORATORY INVESTIGATION, WAS DONE WHICH DIAGNOSED THE BOWEL INJURY. IT IS UNKNOWN WHICH PART OF THE BOWEL WAS INJURED. AN UNSPECIFIED LAPAROSCOPIC PROCEDURE WAS PERFORMED ON THE PATIENT, AND INTRA-OPERATIVELY A SMALL HOLE WAS FOUND IN THE BOWEL. THE BOWEL INJURY WAS REPAIRED WITH SUTURES. NO FURTHER INFORMATION WAS PROVIDED ABOUT THE COURSE OF EVENTS AFTER THE LAPAROSCOPIC PROCEDURE BY THE GENERAL SURGEONS, THE NAME AND MODE (NON-ROBOTIC) OF SURGERY FOR THE SUBSEQUENT PROCEDURE BY THE COLORECTAL SURGEONS, THE CAUSE OF THE INSUFFICIENT ANASTOMOSIS, THE DATE OF DEATH AND CAUSE OF DEATH. NO VIDEOS/IMAGES OF THE PROCEDURE ARE AVAILABLE TO ISI FOR REVIEW. THERE ARE NO INSTRUMENTS AVAILABLE FOR RETURN TO ISI AS THERE IS NO ALLEGATION OF A MALFUNCTION/PRODUCT ISSUES DURING THE DA VINCI PROCEDURE.
BASED ON THE CURRENT INFORMATION PROVIDED, THE CAUSES OF THE POST-OPERATIVE COMPLICATION AND THE PATIENT'S SUBSEQUENT DEATH CANNOT BE DETERMINED. THERE IS NO ALLEGATION THAT A MALFUNCTION OF A DA VINCI SYSTEM, INSTRUMENT, OR ACCESSORY OCCURRED DURING THE PRIMARY PROCEDURE. THEREFORE, NO PRODUCTS ARE EXPECTED FOR RETURN TO ISI FOR EVALUATION. IF ADDITIONAL INFORMATION IS RECEIVED, A FOLLOW-UP MDR WILL BE SUBMITTED. NO IMAGE OR VIDEO CLIP FOR THE REPORTED EVENT WAS SUBMITTED FOR REVIEW. A REVIEW OF THE INSTRUMENT LOGS HAS BEEN PERFORMED. THERE WERE NO OBSERVED EVENTS IN THE AVAILABLE SYSTEM LOGS THAT WOULD SUGGEST A PRODUCT ISSUE, AND LOGGED EVENTS ARE IN LINE WITH NORMAL SYSTEM FUNCTIONALITY. ADDITIONALLY, ALL REUSABLE INSTRUMENTS USED IN THE CASE WERE USED IN SUBSEQUENT PROCEDURES AND A SITE REVIEW SHOWS NO COMPLAINT FILED AGAINST THE INSTRUMENTS. A REVIEW OF THE SYSTEM LOGS HAS BEEN PERFORMED BY AN ISI TECHNICAL SUPPORT ENGINEER (TSE) AND THE FOLLOWING WAS OBSERVED: MULTIPLE PROCEDURES HAVE BEEN PERFORMED ON THIS SYSTEM AFTER THIS REPORTED COMPLAINT WITH NO SERVICE REQUESTS CREATED. NO RELEVANT ERRORS WERE OBSERVED DURING THIS PROCEDURE. HOWEVER, THERE WERE MULTIPLE ERBE GENERATOR RELATED ERRORS SEEN IN THE LOGS. A REVIEW OF THE EVENT WAS CONDUCTED BY AN ISI MEDICAL OFFICER AND THE FOLLOWING ADDITIONAL INFORMATION WAS PROVIDED: BASED UPON THE INFORMATION IN THE DESCRIPTION OF EVENTS, THE BOWEL INJURY THAT OCCURRED DURING THE DA VINCI-ASSISTED HYSTERECTOMY WAS LIKELY CAUSED OR CONTRIBUTED DURING THE USE OF THE DA VINCI SYSTEM AND/OR INSTRUMENTATIONS. HOWEVER, IT IS NOT CLEAR FROM THE DESCRIPTION OF EVENTS AS TO THE ETIOLOGY OF THE BOWEL INJURY THAT ULTIMATELY LED TO THE PATIENTS MORTALITY. THIS COMPLAINT IS BEING REPORTED DUE TO THE FOLLOWING CONCLUSION: AFTER A SUCCESSFUL DA VINCI-ASSISTED HYSTERECTOMY PROCEDURE, THE PATIENT COMPLAINED OF ABDOMINAL PAIN 5-7 DAYS POST-PROCEDURE. SHE WAS FOUND TO HAVE A BOWEL INJURY AND UNDERWENT A LAPAROSCOPIC PROCEDURE. A HOLE IN THE BOWEL WAS DISCOVERED WHICH WAS SUTURED. A FEW DAYS LATER, THE PATIENT'S CONDITION DETERIORATED AND SHE UNDERWENT ANOTHER UNSPECIFIED PROCEDURE WHEREBY THE COLORECTAL SURGEONS DISCOVERED HER ABDOMEN WAS INFECTED DUE TO AN "INSUFFICIENT ANASTOMOSIS." THE PATIENT SUBSEQUENTLY PASSED AWAY ON AN UNSPECIFIED DATE. THE CAUSES OF THE POST-OPERATIVE COMPLICATION AND THE PATIENT'S SUBSEQUENT DEMISE ARE UNKNOWN. THERE IS NO ALLEGATION OF SYSTEM, INSTRUMENT OR ACCESSORY MALFUNCTION DURING THE PROCEDURE AND NO ALLEGATION THAT THE DA VINCI SYSTEM CONTRIBUTED TO PATIENT HARM AND DEATH.
ADDITIONAL INFORMATION CAN BE FOUND IN THE FOLLOWING SECTIONS: G3, G6, H2, AND H10. ON 23-SEP-2022, INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC. (ISI) RECEIVED THE FOLLOWING ADDITIONAL INFORMATION ABOUT THE REPORTED EVENT: AN ISI FIELD SERVICE ENGINEER (FSE) WAS DISPATCHED TO THE CUSTOMER SITE TO FURTHER INVESTIGATE THE REPORTED COMPLAINT. THE ERBE GENERATOR WAS REPLACED PROACTIVELY BASED ON A REVIEW OF THE SYSTEM LOGS. THE SYSTEM WAS TESTED AND VERIFIED AS READY FOR USE. THE PRODUCT HAS NOT BEEN RETURNED FOR EVALUATION. THE SYSTEM LOGS WERE REVIEWED BY AN ISI ADVANCED FAILURE ANALYSIS ENGINEER (FAE) AND THE FOLLOWING FINDINGS WERE OBSERVED REGARDING THE ERBE RELATED ERRORS:  ERROR 25920 INDICATE ENERGY ACTIVATION WAS STOPPED, AND THIS OCCURRED ON A BIPOLAR PORT, SUGGESTING THE ISSUE INVOLVED THE MARYLAND BIPOLAR INSTRUMENT.  ERROR 25913 INDICATES AN ENERGY ACTIVATION WAS ATTEMPTED WHILE AN ENERGY INSTRUMENT WAS NOT INSTALLED ON THE UNIVERSAL SURGICAL MANIPULATOR (USM). THE ERBE SHOULD NOT DELIVER ENERGY IF THE INSTRUMENT IS NOT INSTALLED ON A USM. THE FAE WAS UNABLE TO DETERMINE IF THESE ERRORS CONTRIBUTED TO ANY CAUTERY INSTRUMENT ISSUES OR IF THE ERRORS CONTRIBUTED TO THE REPORTED INJURY. THE CAUTERY INSTRUMENTS (MONOPOLAR CURVED SCISSORS AND MARYLAND BIPOLAR) USED IN THE PROCEDURE WERE RE-USED IN SUBSEQUENT PROCEDURES.

ALL OPERATIONS ARE PERFORMED BY THREE GYNECOLOGICAL ONCOLOGISTS IN OUR CENTER, ALL OF WHOM HAVE NEARLY 20 YEARS OF EXPERIENCE IN MINIMALLY INVASIVE SURGERY AND HAVE RECEIVED PROFESSIONAL TRAINING TO OBTAIN OPERATING QUALIFICATIONS. OUR DEPARTMENT HAS ALWAYS USED THE DA VINCI ROBOT SI SYSTEM. OTHER RELATED INFORMATION FROM THE ARTICLE IS AS FOLLOWS: THERE WERE NO PATIENT DEATHS RELATED TO SURGICAL COMPLICATIONS. FROM OUR FINDINGS, PATIENT MORTALITY WAS CORRELATED WITH TUMOR STAGE, AND THERE WERE NO PATIENT DEATHS RELATED TO SURGICAL COMPLICATIONS. DETAILS OF THE PATIENTS WHO DIED AND RELAPSED HAVE BEEN DESCRIBED IN TABLE 5 IN THE ARTICLE, ALL OF WHOM DIED FROM DISEASE PROGRESSION (RECURRENCE OR METASTASIS). DETAILS OF THE PATIENTS WHO DIED AND RELAPSED, INCLUDING DISEASE-FREE SURVIVAL TIME (DFS WAS DEFINED AS THE DATE FROM SURGERY TO THE DIAGNOSIS OF RECURRENCE ON IMAGING EXAMINATION OR TISSUE BIOPSY, OR THE LAST FOLLOW-UP) AND OVERALL SURVIVAL TIME (OS REFERRED TO THE DATE FROM SURGERY TO DEATH OR THE LAST FOLLOW-UP) HAVE BEEN DESCRIBED IN TABLE 5 IN THE ARTICLE. FROM OUR FINDINGS, PATIENT MORTALITY WAS CORRELATED WITH TUMOR STAGE. SOME STUDIES HAVE ALSO INDICATED THAT HISTOLOGIC CELL TYPE ALSO IMPACTED SURVIVAL, AND THAT RARE HISTOLOGIC CELL TYPE MIGHT HAVE A WORSE PROGNOSIS."

A VASCULAR SURGEON AND GENERAL SURGEON WERE BROUGHT IN TO ADDRESS THE BLEEDING. THE PATIENT WAS IN THE ICU AND THEN TAKEN BACK TO THE OR FOR AN UNKNOWN REASON. THE PATIENT SUBSEQUENT EXPIRED ON AN UNSPECIFIED DATE/TIME. THE CAUSES OF THE REPORTED UNSPECIFIED VESSEL INJURY AND THE PATIENTS SUBSEQUENT DEATH ARE UNKNOWN. THERE IS NO ALLEGATION THAT AN ISI DEVICE MALFUNCTIONED, THE CAUSE OF THE VESSEL INJURY AND DEATH IS UNKNOWN.
IT WAS REPORTED THAT DURING A DA VINCI-ASSISTED MEDIAN ARCUATE LIGAMENT RELEASE PROCEDURE, THE CASE WAS CONVERTED TO OPEN SURGERY. AN UNSPECIFIED TIME LATER, THE PATIENT REPORTEDLY PASSED AWAY. (ISI) CONTACTED THE ISI CLINICAL SALES REPRESENTATIVE (CSR) AND OBTAINED THE FOLLOWING ADDITIONAL INFORMATION REGARDING THE REPORTED EVENT: THE CSR WAS INFORMED BY A NURSE AT THE SITE THAT DURING THE PROCEDURE, THE SURGEON WAS DISSECTING THE AORTA AND CELIAC ARTERY, AND THE PATIENT EXPERIENCED BLEEDING. IT IS UNKNOWN WHAT SPECIFIC INSTRUMENTS THE SURGEON USED WHILE PERFORMING DISSECTION. THE CSR STATED HE WAS UNSURE WHAT SPECIFIC VESSEL WAS BLEEDING. THE SURGEON TRIED TO SUTURE THE INJURED VESSEL. THE CONSOLE SURGEON HAD A VASCULAR AND A GENERAL SURGEON BROUGHT IN TO ADDRESS THE BLEEDING. IT IS UNKNOWN WHAT MEDICAL/SURGICAL INTERVENTION WAS RENDERED DUE TO THE BLEEDING. THE PATIENT WAS IN THE ICU AND THEN BROUGHT BACK TO THE OR FOR AN UNKNOWN REASON. THE PATIENT SUBSEQUENTLY EXPIRED. ACCORDING TO THE CSR, THE NURSE INDICATED THAT THERE IS NO ALLEGATION THAT A MALFUNCTION OF A DA VINCI SYSTEM, INSTRUMENT, OR ACCESSORY OCCURRED. ISI MADE MULTIPLE FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPTS TO OBTAIN ADDITIONAL INFORMATION FROM THE SURGEON. HOWEVER, NO FURTHER DETAILS HAVE BEEN RECEIVED AS OF THE DATE OF THIS REPORT.

(ISI) FOR FAILURE ANALYSIS EVALUATION. THE PATIENT HAD CARDIOVASCULAR DISEASE DUE TO A SMOKING HISTORY AND THIS MADE CONTROL OF THE BLEEDING DIFFICULT. ADDITIONALLY, THE LEVEL OF SURGEON EXPERIENCE IN PERFORMING AN MEDIAN ARCUATE LIGAMENT RELEASE IS UNKNOWN. THE ADVERSE EVENT DESCRIBED IS NOT UNIQUE TO AN ISI PRODUCT OR PROCEDURE. AN ISI FIELD SERVICE ENGINEER (FSE) WAS DISPATCHED TO THE CUSTOMER SITE TO FURTHER INVESTIGATE THE REPORTED COMPLAINT: THE FSE INSPECTED THE SYSTEM AND FOUND THE GRIP PAD ON THE RIGHT MASTER TOOL MANIPULATOR (MTM) WAS DAMAGED. AN EVENT CODE 1126 ON UNIVERSAL SURGICAL MANIPULATOR (USM) 2 WAS IDENTIFIED. THE GRIP PAD ON THE RIGHT MTM WAS REPLACED. TO CORRECT THE EVENT CODE 1126, THE USM2 WAS REPLACED. THE SYSTEM PASSED ALL REQUIRED TESTS. THE SYSTEM WAS NOTED TO BE WORKING PROPERLY.
THE UNIVERSAL SURGICAL MANIPULATOR (USM) THAT WAS REPLACED DURING A PROACTIVE FIELD VISIT DUE TO THE DEATH REPORTED WAS RECEIVED AND FAILURE ANALYSIS WAS COMPLETED. PER THE INVESTIGATION, THE UNIT CAME INTO FAILURE ANALYSIS WITH REPORTED PROBLEM OF "1126 ON USM2," AND WAS CONFIRMED AS HAVING OCCURRED IN THE FIELD VIA THE SYSTEM LOGS. THE UNIT WAS TESTED ON AN IN-HOUSE SYSTEM IN NORMAL MODE WHERE IT PINGED AN 1126 LOW FIBER ERROR. THE UNIT WAS THEN TESTED ON A PFTP WHERE IT FAILED THE FIBER TEST ON THE PARALLELOGRAM. A GOLD PARALLELOGRAM WAS INSTALLED, AND THE ERROR DISAPPEARED. THE ORIGINAL PARALLELOGRAM FIBER WAS INSTALLED, AND THE FAILURE RETURNED. THE PARALLELOGRAM FIBER ASSEMBLY WOULD BE REPLACED AS A FIX.

IT WAS DETERMINED HE HAD A HERNIA AND HE WAS SENT TO A DOCTOR TO HAVE IT REPAIRED. FDA SAFETY REPORT ID # (B)(4)." PER THE FDA VOLUNTARY REPORT, THE INITIAL REPORTER ELECTED NOT TO BE IDENTIFIED. IT WAS REPORTED THAT DURING A DA VINCI-ASSISTED ESOPHAGECTOMY TRANSTHORACIC-CHEST ANASTOMOSIS PROCEDURE, DURING THE BEGINNING OF THE THORACIC PART (DOCKING WAS ALREADY COMPLETED, AND THE SURGERY IN PROGRESS), AN IDIOPATHIC BLEEDING CAME UP WHICH WAS CONTROLLED WITH THE CADIERE FORCEPS INSTRUMENT. THE SYNCHROSEAL AND THE TIP-UP GRASPER INSTRUMENTS WERE THE OTHER INSTRUMENTS INSTALLED AT THAT TIME. THE BLEEDING WAS CONTROLLED AND WAS NOTED TO BE POTENTIALLY DUE TO THE INJURY OF AN INTERCOSTAL VESSEL THAT WAS DESCRIBED AS NOT CRITICAL. APPROXIMATELY FIVE MINUTES AFTER THIS BLEEDING EVENT, THE PATIENT SUDDENLY WENT INTO ASYSTOLE. THE SURGEON, TOGETHER WITH THE CARDIAC SURGEON AND THORACIC SURGEON, DECIDED TO CONVERT THE PROCEDURE TO OPEN. FURTHER "NON-DA VINCI-RELATED COMPLICATIONS" OCCURRED RESULTING IN AORTIC INJURY. THE SURGERY WAS COMPLETED, BUT THE PATIENT DIED IN THE EVENING. INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC. (ISI) HAS ATTEMPTED TO OBTAIN ADDITIONAL INFORMATION. HOWEVER, AS OF THE DATE OF THIS REPORT, NO FURTHER DETAILS HAVE BEEN RECEIVED.

IT WAS REPORTED THAT DURING A DA VINCI-ASSISTED CHOLECYSTECTOMY PROCEDURE, THE AORTA WAS DAMAGED WHILE A TROCAR WAS BEING PLACED. THE PROCEDURE WAS REPORTEDLY CONVERTED TO OPEN AND THE PATIENT WAS IN THE ICU LATER THAT DAY RECOVERING. HOWEVER, THE NEXT DAY IT WAS REPORTED THAT THE PATIENT HAD EXPIRED. AN INTUITIVE SURGICAL INC. (ISI) CLINICAL SALES ASSOCIATE (CSA), WHO WAS PRESENT DURING THIS PROCEDURE, WAS CONTACTED AND ADDITIONAL INFORMATION WAS OBTAINED ABOUT THE COMPLAINT: THE SURGEON WAS NEW TO THIS HOSPITAL, BUT AN EXPERIENCED DA VINCI SURGEON. THE SURGEON OF THIS PROCEDURE HAD A SURGEON FROM THE HOSPITAL PROCTORING THIS CASE AS THE HOSPITAL REQUIRES ALL NEW SURGEONS TO BE PROCTORED. THE SURGEON OF THIS PROCEDURE WAS ABOUT TO PLACE THEIR FIRST TROCAR OF THE PROCEDURE. THE PROCTOR SUGGESTED PLACING A 5MM TROCAR WITH A LAPAROSCOPIC CAMERA TO START, BUT THE SURGEON OF THE PROCEDURE PLACED A 12MM TROCAR AT MIDLINE FOR THE INITIAL TROCAR ENTRY. THE 12MM TROCAR WAS NOT A DA VINCI BRAND TROCAR, BUT THE SURGEON DID USE A DA VINCI ENDOSCOPE IN THE TROCAR TO VISUALIZE THEIR ENTRY. THE SURGEON PLACED THIS 12MM TROCAR, NOTICED SOME BLEEDING, BUT CONTINUED AND BEGAN INSUFFLATION. THE SURGEON PLACED ANOTHER TROCAR AND NOTICED THE BLEEDING WAS INCREASING AND CONVERTED THE PROCEDURE TO OPEN. THE CSA LEFT THE ROOM AT THIS POINT. THE CSA SAID THE DA VINCI SYSTEM WAS NEVER DOCKED WHILE HE WAS PRESENT AND THE PATIENT WAS OPENED DUE TO THE BLEEDING WHEN HE LEFT THE ROOM. THE ISI EXECUTIVE SALES REPRESENTATIVE (ESR) WAS INFORMED VIA TEXT BY THE HOSPITAL OR MANAGER THAT THE PATIENT WAS RECOVERING IN THE ICU LATER ON THE DAY OF THE PROCEDURE. IN AN ADDITIONAL COMMUNICATION BY THE SITE OR MANAGER, THE ESR WAS TOLD THAT THE PATIENT HAD EXPIRED. THE ISI ESR WAS CONTACTED AND ADDITIONAL INFORMATION WAS OBTAINED ABOUT THE COMPLAINT: THE SURGEON OF THIS PROCEDURE HAS BEEN PERFORMING PROCEDURES SINCE THIS EVENT OCCURRED. THE SURGEON TOLD THE ESR THAT THEY THOUGHT THIS WAS A FREAK ACCIDENT BUT NO OTHER DISCUSSION WAS HAD. THE ESR WAS TOLD BY PEOPLE IN THE RECOVERY WARD THAT THE AORTA WAS DAMAGED AND THE BLEEDING COULD NOT BE CONTROLLED, SO THE AORTA WAS CLAMPED TO STOP THE BLOOD FLOW. THE PATIENT WAS TAKEN TO THE ICU AND LATER EXPIRED, THE SAME DAY THAT THIS EVENT OCCURRED. ISI HAS ATTEMPTED TO OBTAIN ADDITIONAL INFORMATION FROM THE SURGEON OF THIS PROCEDURE. HOWEVER, AS OF THE DATE OF THIS REPORT, NO FURTHER DETAILS HAVE BEEN RECEIVED.
REFER TO H10/H11 FOR FOLLOW-UP INFORMATION.
A MEDICAL REVIEW OF THE EVENT INFORMATION WAS PERFORMED BY AN INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC. (ISI) MEDICAL SAFETY OFFICER (MSO) AND THE FOLLOWING INFORMATION WAS PROVIDED: BASED UPON THE INFORMATION IN THE DESCRIPTION OF EVENTS, WHILE GAINING ACCESS TO THE ABDOMINAL CAVITY FOR A DA VINCI-ASSISTED CHOLECYSTECTOMY, THE AORTA WAS INJURED DURING INITIAL PLACEMENT OF A 12 MM THIRD PARTY TROCAR USING A DIRECT VISUAL ACCESS TECHNIQUE. ACCORDING TO THE SOCIETY OF LAPAROSCOPIC & ROBOTIC SURGEONS ARTICLE ENTITLED, LAPAROSCOPIC TROCAR COMPLICATIONS, BY RONALD A. RHODES, MD, FACS, HTTPS://SLS.ORG/THE-3RD-EDITION-PREVENTION-MANAGEMENT/CHAPTER-10/, MAJOR VASCULAR INJURY RATES DURING INITIAL ENTRY ARE BETWEEN 0.05% AND 0.5%. THE MOST COMMON SITES OF INJURY ARE THE ILIAC VEIN, GREATER OMENTAL VESSELS, INFERIOR VENA CAVA, AORTA, PELVIC AND SUPERIOR MESENTERIC VEINS, AND LUMBAR VEINS.

IT WAS REPORTED THAT AFTER A DA VINCI ASSISTED BILATERAL INGUINAL HERNIA REPAIR PROCEDURE, ON POST-OPERATIVE DAY #2, THE PATIENT EXPIRED. FROM AVAILABLE INFORMATION, THE PATIENT WAS DISCHARGED IN STABLE CONDITION THE DAY FOLLOWING THE PROCEDURE. A DAY AFTER HE WAS RELEASED, THE PATIENT EXPIRED AT HOME. INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC. (ISI) CONTACTED THE INVOLVED SURGEON AND OBTAINED THE FOLLOWING ADDITIONAL INFORMATION REGARDING THE REPORTED EVENT: ON (B)(6) 2022, THE PATIENT UNDERWENT A DA VINCI-ASSISTED BILATERAL INGUINAL HERNIA PROCEDURE WITH NO INTRAOPERATIVE COMPLICATIONS. THERE WAS NO BLOOD LOSS AND NO COMPLICATIONS OBSERVED. OTHER PROVIDED INFORMATION INDICATED THAT WHILE THE SURGEON WAS PEELING THE PERITONEUM, THERE WAS MINOR OOZE FROM THE CAPILLARIES, FOR WHICH THE SURGEON USED HEMOSTATIC GEL TO ADDRESS IT. AS THE CAPILLARIES WERE SO TINY, THE SURGEON DID NOT WANT TO USE ENERGY. THE SURGEON REPORTED THAT HE HAD EVEN COMMENTED TO THE OPERATING ROOM (OR) STAFF THAT THIS WAS ONE OF THE CLEANEST AND MOST SMOOTH INGUINAL HERNIA REPAIRS. THE PATIENT WAS TREATED WITH ULTRAM AND FLEXERIL FOR POSTOPERATIVE PAIN AND MUSCLE RELAXANT. ON POSTOPERATIVE DAY #1, THE PATIENT WAS DISCHARGED WITH NO POSTOPERATIVE ISSUES AND WAS VERY STABLE. ON POSTOPERATIVE DAY #2 ((B)(6) 2022), THE PATIENT HAD NOT REPORTED ANY SYMPTOMS OR SIGNS OF ANY ISSUES. THE SURGEON WAS INFORMED THAT THE PATIENT'S WIFE HAD FOUND HER HUSBAND DEAD. THE SURGEON REPORTED THE PATIENT WAS A 48-YEAR-OLD MALE WITH A HISTORY OF NARCOTIC ABUSE. THE SURGEON DOES NOT BELIEVE THE PATIENT HAD ANY BLEEDING DISORDERS. THE PATIENT HAD MANY TATTOOS AND REPORTED NO BLEEDING ISSUES WITH THAT. PRE-OPERATIVELY THE PATIENT WAS ON LOVENOX (ANTICOAGULANT). THE SURGEON DID NOT KNOW IF THE PATIENT HAD A PROTEIN C AND S DEFICIENCY. THE SURGEON REPORTED THAT THE AUTOPSY RESULT STATED THE PATIENT'S DEATH WAS DUE TO SLOW VENOUS BLOOD LOSS. NO SOURCE IDENTIFIED ANY SPECIFIC VESSEL WITH EVIDENT VESSEL DAMAGE. THE SURGEON REITERATED THAT THERE WAS NO ISI DEVICE OR SYSTEM MALFUNCTION DURING THE PROCEDURE. AS A RESULT, THERE IS NO NECESSITY FOR ANY OF THE PRODUCTS TO BE RETURNED.

THE PATIENT IS SUSPECTED TO HAVE SUBSEQUENTLY DIED DUE TO SEPSIS AND RENAL FAILURE. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OR ALLEGATION THAT AN ISI DEVICE CAUSED OR CONTRIBUTED TO AN ADVERSE EVENT OR THAT AN ISI DEVICE MALFUNCTIONED IN A WAY THAT COULD CONTRIBUTE TO AN ADVERSE EVENT IF IT WERE TO RECUR. IT WAS REPORTED THAT AFTER A DA VINCI-ASSISTED DUODENAL SWITCH BARIATRIC SURGICAL PROCEDURE, THE PATIENT EXPIRED. THE PATIENT UNDERWENT A SECOND PROCEDURE AND THEN PASSED AWAY. THERE IS NO ALLEGATION OF A MALFUNCTION OF AN INTUITIVE PRODUCT AND SYSTEM. INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC. (ISI) CONTACTED THE BARIATRIC MANAGER AT THE SITE AND THE SURGICAL TECHNICIAN WHO WAS IN THE ROOM AND OBTAINED THE FOLLOWING INFORMATION. THE BARIATRIC MANAGER STATED THAT SHE TALKED TO THE SURGEON ABOUT THE EVENT. IT WAS REPORTED THAT UPON FIRST PORT ENTRY WITH A LAPAROSCOPIC THIRD PARTY (ETHICON) TROCAR, THE SURGEON THOUGHT HE HAD INJURED THE COLON. AS A RESULT, HE CONTINUED LAPAROSCOPICALLY, SEARCHED FOR AN HOUR, AND CONFIRMED THERE WAS NO COLON INJURY. THEN THE ROBOT WAS DOCKED, AND THE SURGEON CONTINUED WITH THE PROCEDURE. DURING THE ROBOTIC PROCEDURE, THE SURGEON IDENTIFIED THAT THE PATIENT HAD A SIGNIFICANT HIATAL HERNIA WHICH HE WAS UNAWARE OF AS IT WAS NOT DOCUMENTED IN THE UPPER ENDOSCOPY RESULTS. DUE TO THE HIATAL HERNIA, THE SURGEON ELECTED TO CONVERT THE ROBOTIC PROCEDURE TO A TRADITIONAL LAPAROSCOPIC PROCEDURE. AFTER ADDRESSING THE HIATAL HERNIA, THE SURGEON SWITCHED BACK TO THE DA VINCI SURGICAL SYSTEM AND COMPLETED THE PLANNED PROCEDURE ROBOTICALLY. THE SURGEON USUALLY LEAVES ONE DRAIN IN THE PATIENT AS A STANDARD PRACTICE. HOWEVER, AS A PRECAUTION, THE SURGEON LEFT TWO DRAINS IN THE PATIENT AND KEPT THE PATIENT FOR TWO ADDITIONAL DAYS IN THE HOSPITAL FOR OBSERVATION. AFTER CONFIRMATION THAT THE DRAINS WERE CLEAR, THE PATIENT WAS DISCHARGED HOME. THE PATIENT THEN WENT BACK TO HIS HOMETOWN ON AN UNSPECIFIED DATE. THE NEXT MORNING THE PATIENT EXPERIENCED ABDOMINAL PAIN AND TENDERNESS. THE PATIENT WENT TO ANOTHER HOSPITAL AND UNDERWENT AN EXPLORATORY LAPAROSCOPIC PROCEDURE BY A DIFFERENT SURGEON. THE SURGEON IDENTIFIED A COLON INJURY. AS A RESULT, A COLOSTOMY WAS PERFORMED AS A REPAIR. THE PATIENT WAS LEFT OPEN WITH A WOUND VAC. THE SEQUENCE OF EVENTS IS UNCLEAR; HOWEVER, IT IS BELIEVED THE PATIENT DIED OF SEPSIS AND RENAL FAILURE. THE DATE OF DEATH WAS (B)(6) 2022. ISI CONTACTED THE SITES DIRECTOR OF QUALITY AND PATIENT SAFETY. THE DIRECTOR OF QUALITY AND PATIENT SAFETY STATED THAT THEIR SITE HAS NO INFORMATION FROM THE SECOND SITE WHERE THE PATIENT THE SUBSEQUENT EXPLORATORY LAPAROSCOPIC PROCEDURE. THE DIRECTOR OF QUALITY AND PATIENT SAFETY STATED THE COLON INJURY WAS NOT DUE TO THE PLACEMENT OF THE TROCAR OR A DEFECTIVE LAPAROSCOPIC TROCAR, AND IT WAS UNRELATED TO THE ROBOT. HOWEVER, THERE IS NO CONFIRMATION ON HOW AND WHEN THE COLON INJURY OCCURRED.

IT WAS REPORTED THAT DURING A PLANNED DA VINCI-ASSISTED BENIGN HYSTERECTOMY PROCEDURE, THE SURGEON ACCIDENTALLY NICKED THE AORTA WITH AN UNSPECIFIED TROCAR. ISI CONTACTED THE ISI CLINICAL SALES REPRESENTATIVE (CSR) AND OBTAINED THE FOLLOWING INFORMATION REGARDING THE REPORTED EVENT: ON 02-AUG-2022, THE CSR WAS INFORMED OF THE PATIENTS DEATH. IT WAS REPORTED THAT DURING A DA VINCI-ASSISTED RIGHT HEMI-HEPATECTOMY PROCEDURE, A PATIENT DIED. THE EVENT OCCURRED AFTER THE ROBOTICS PORTION OF THE PROCEDURE WAS COMPLETED AND THE RIGHT HEPATIC VEIN HAD TO BE REMOVED. THE HEPATIC VEIN REMOVAL WAS NOT CONDUCTED WITH THE DA VINCI SYSTEM BECAUSE ROBOTIC WHITE STAPLER RELOADS WERE UNAVAILABLE. THE SURGEON ELECTED TO USE AN ECHELON 45MM STAPLER (NOT AN INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC. (ISI) DEVICE) WITH A WHITE RELOAD. IT IS UNCLEAR IF THE SURGEON USED A THIRD-PARTY TROCAR OR A PREVIOUSLY PLACED DA VINCI TROCAR TO INSET THE STAPLER INSTRUMENT. UPON OPENING THE ECHELON 45 STAPLER JAWS, MASSIVE VENOUS BLEEDING OCCURRED. THE CAUSE WAS A 4 CM LONGITUDINAL DEFECT IN THE INFERIOR VENA CAVA EXTENDING TO THE DIAPHRAGM. THE SURGEON MADE A 3-MINUTE ATTEMPT TO SUTURE THE DEFECT WITH ROBOTIC ASSISTANCE. DUE TO POOR VISIBILITY, THE SURGEON MADE THE CLINICAL DECISION TO CONVERT TO OPEN. ONCE OPEN, THE SURGEON BEGAN TO SUTURE THE VENA CAVA FOLLOWED. WHILE ATTEMPTING TO REPAIR THE VENA CAVA, THE PATIENT WENT INTO CARDIAC ARREST. ATTEMPTS TO RESUSCITATE THE PATIENT WERE UNSUCCESSFUL. PER THE SURGEON, THE CAUSE OF THE ERROR CANNOT BE DETERMINED. THE SURGEON DOES NOT BELIEVE A MALFUNCTION OF THE DA VINCI SYSTEM, INSTRUMENT, OR ACCESSORY OCCURRED. PER THE SURGEON, THE CAUSE OF THE ERROR CANNOT BE DETERMINED. THE SURGEON SUSPECTS THE VENA CAVA WAS INJURED DURING THE INSERTION OF THE ECHELON 45 STAPLER; HOWEVER, NO BLEEDING WAS OBSERVED DURING THIS MANIPULATION. THE SURGEON ALSO SUSPECTS THAT WHEN CLOSING THE ECHELON 45 STAPLER JAWS, TRACTION OR GRAVITY FORCES WERE RELEASED, WHICH LED TO A RUPTURE OF THE VESSEL; HOWEVER, NO BLEEDING WAS OBSERVED AFTER CLOSING THE INSTRUMENT. PER THE SURGEON, A THIRD POSSIBILITY COULD BE THAT THE ECHELON 45 STAPLER DID NOT WORK SUFFICIENTLY; HOWEVER, THIS COULD NOT BE CONFIRMED DUE TO THE POOR VISIBILITY THAT FOLLOWED THE MASSIVE VENOUS BLEEDING EVENT AND COULD NOT BE ACCESSED WHETHER A SUFFICIENT ROW OF STAPLES COULD BE SEEN ON THE VENA CAVA. THE CAUSE OF DEATH AS IT APPEARS ON THE DEATH CERTIFICATE IS LISTED AS HEMORRHAGIC SHOCK WITH IRREVERSIBLE DAMAGE TO THE HEART AND CENTRAL NERVOUS SYSTEM DUE TO INTRAOPERATIVE INJURY TO THE INFERIOR VENA CAVA WITH APPROXIMATELY 10 MINUTES OF ISCHEMIA. THE OPERATION WAS RECORDED ON VIDEO; HOWEVER, THE RECORDING HAS BEEN SEIZED BY THE PUBLIC PROSECUTORS OFFICE AND IS UNAVAILABLE FOR ISI TO REVIEW.

ON (B)(6) 2022, INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC. (ISI) BECAME AWARE OF A JOURNAL OF SURGICAL ONCOLOGY ARTICLE TITLED, LAPAROSCOPIC VERSUS ROBOTIC ADRENALECTOMY IN PHEOCHROMOCYTOMA PATIENTS (ISIKTAS, G., AVCI, S., ET AL., 2022). STUDY DATA WAS RETROACTIVELY COLLECTED FROM MARCH 2000 THROUGH OCTOBER 2021 WITH A TOTAL OF 157 PATIENTS (RA: N=87, LA: N=70). THE PURPOSE OF THE STUDY WAS TO COMPARE PERIOPERATIVE OUTCOMES OF LAPAROSCOPIC ADRENALECTOMY (LA) VERSUS ROBOTIC ADRENALECTOMY (RA) FOR PHEOCHROMOCYTOMA. WITHIN THE JOURNAL ARTICLE, A CONVERSION INVOLVING A DA VINCI SURGICAL PROCEDURE IS NOTED. PER THE ARTICLE, THERE WAS ONE CONVERSION TO OPEN SURGERY IN THE RA GROUP DUE TO AN INABILITY TO OBTAIN ADEQUATE SURGICAL EXPOSURE AND MAKE PROGRESS DURING THE CASE. ADDITIONALLY, WITHIN THE JOURNAL ARTICLE, POST-OPERATIVE COMPLICATIONS OCCURRED IN 4 PATIENTS FROM THE RA GROUP. COMPLICATIONS INCLUDED URINARY TRACT INFECTIONS (N=2), PNEUMONIA (N=1) AND VOLUME OVERLOAD (N=1). PATIENTS WERE FOLLOWED UP FOR A MEDIAN OF 60 MONTHS. DURING THIS FOLLOW-UP, ONE PATIENT DEVELOPED A RECURRENCE INVOLVING THE OPPOSITE ADRENAL GLAND AND ANOTHER PATIENT HAD A MULTIFOCAL SYSTEMIC RECURRENCE IN THE ROBOTIC GROUP. THERE WAS ONE PHEOCHROMOCYTOMA-RELATED DEATH IN THE ROBOTIC GROUP INVOLVING A PATIENT WHO DEVELOPED SYSTEMIC METASTASES. FOR THE PATIENT REFERENCED IN THE ARTICLE WITH THE REPORTED CONVERSION TO OPEN IN THE RA GROUP, THE AUTHOR REPORTED THAT THE EVENT WAS NOT RELATED TO THE DA VINCI SYSTEM AND THE SYSTEM WORKED WELL. THE AUTHOR EXPLAINED THAT IT WAS RELATED TO A TUMOR WITH INFILTRATION INTO SURROUNDING TISSUES MAKING THE PROGRESS DIFFICULT. PER THE AUTHOR, SOMETIMES, WE HAVE DIFFICULT CASES IN CHALLENGING TUMORS AND PATIENTS. THERE WAS NO REPORTED PATIENT HARM OR INJURY. PER THE AUTHOR, THERE WERE NO COMPLICATIONS AND NO ADVERSE EVENTS. ALSO, THE AUTHOR INDICATED THAT THERE WAS NO MALFUNCTION OF A DEVICE.

THE INITIAL REPORTER CLAIMED THAT THE PATIENT'S TREATMENT WAS NEGATIVELY AFFECTED BY THE DA VINCI-ASSISTED SURGERY BY ALLEGEDLY STIRRING UP AND SPREADING THE CANCER WHICH EXPEDITED HER DEATH.

ACCORDING TO THE INITIAL REPORTER, THE PATIENT'S TREATMENT WAS NEGATIVELY AFFECTED BY THE DA VINCI-ASSISTED SURGERY. IT WAS REPORTED THAT AFTER UNDERGOING A DA VINCI-ASSISTED GASTRIC BYPASS SURGICAL PROCEDURE, THE PATIENT DEVELOPED A LEAK, WAS ADMITTED, AND SUBSEQUENTLY PASSED AWAY. THE DATE OF THE ROBOTIC PROCEDURE IS UNKNOWN. THE PATIENT POST-OPERATIVELY UNDERWENT A SECOND SURGICAL PROCEDURE PERFORMED NON-ROBOTICALLY FOR WHAT WAS BELIEVED TO BE AN INCARCERATED HERNIA. AT THAT TIME, IT WAS IDENTIFIED THAT "SOMETHING LEAKING LED TO SEPSIS." THE ROBOTIC SURGEON WAS OUT OF TOWN, SO HIS PARTNER PERFORMED THE SECOND SURGICAL PROCEDURE NON-ROBOTICALLY. DUE TO SEPSIS, THE PATIENT EXPIRED WITHIN 24 HOURS OF THE SECOND SURGICAL PROCEDURE FOR THE INCARCERATED HERNIA REPAIR. THE SURGEON HAD STATED THAT THE INCARCERATED HERNIA THAT LED TO THE PATIENT'S DEMISE WAS UNRELATED TO THE ROBOT. INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC. (ISI) MADE MULTIPLE FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPTS TO OBTAIN ADDITIONAL INFORMATION. HOWEVER, NO FURTHER DETAILS HAVE BEEN RECEIVED AS OF THE DATE OF THIS REPORT.
BASED ON THE INFORMATION PROVIDED, ALTHOUGH THE CAUSE OF THE DEATH IS DUE TO SEPSIS FROM THE INCARCERATED HERNIA, THE CAUSE OF THE INCARCERATED HERNIA IS UNKNOWN. IF ADDITIONAL INFORMATION IS RECEIVED, A FOLLOW-UP MDR WILL BE SUBMITTED. A REVIEW OF THE SYSTEM LOGS CANNOT BE PERFORMED AT THIS TIME DUE TO LACK OF EVENT DETAILS. THE SURGERY DATES ARE UNKNOWN. A REVIEW OF THE EVENT WAS CONDUCTED BY AN ISI MEDICAL OFFICER, AND THE FOLLOWING ADDITIONAL INFORMATION WAS PROVIDED: THE ADVERSE EVENT DESCRIBED IS NOT UNIQUE TO AN ISI PRODUCT OR PROCEDURE. THE LIMITED INFORMATION DESCRIBES AN INCARCERATED HERNIA THAT WENT ON TO PERFORATE, WHICH LED TO SEPSIS AND ULTIMATELY DEATH. THERE ARE MANY TYPES OF HERNIAS; SOME ARE CONGENITAL, SOME ARE ACQUIRED, AND SOME CAN OCCUR POST OPERATIVELY. THE LOCATION AND TYPE OF HERNIA IS KEY INFORMATION BUT IS NOT SPECIFIED. NOR IS THE PERIOD OF TIME BETWEEN THE INITIAL ROBOTIC PROCEDURE AND THE SECOND PRESENTATION WHEN THE HERNIA WAS DISCOVERED. THESE ARE IMPORTANT DETAILS IN UNDERSTANDING HOW THE HERNIA MAY HAVE OCCURRED AND IF IT WAS RELATED TO THE INITIAL ROBOTIC PROCEDURE. THEREFORE, THERE IS INSUFFICIENT INFORMATION TO DETERMINE IF ANY INTUITIVE PRODUCTS CONTRIBUTED TO THIS PATIENTS DEATH. THIS COMPLAINT IS CONSIDERED A REPORTABLE EVENT DUE TO THE FOLLOWING CONCLUSION: AFTER UNDERGOING A DA VINCI-ASSISTED GASTRIC BYPASS PROCEDURE, THE PATIENT REPORTEDLY EXPERIENCED AN INCARCERATED HERNIA. AS A RESULT, THE UNDERWENT A SECOND SURGICAL PROCEDURE PERFORMED NON-ROBOTICALLY FOR REPAIR OF THE INCARCERATED HERNIA. THE PATIENT EXPIRED WITHIN 24 HOURS OF THE SECOND NON-ROBOTIC PROCEDURE DUE TO SEPSIS. ALTHOUGH THE SURGEON INDICATED THAT THE INCARCERATED HERNIA WAS UNRELATED TO THE ROBOT, THE CAUSE OF THE POST-OPERATIVE COMPLICATION RESULTING WITH SEPSIS AND THE PATIENT'S SUBSEQUENT DEMISE IS UNKNOWN.

IT WAS REPORTED THAT DURING A DA VINCI-ASSISTED ADRENALECTOMY PROCEDURE, THE SPLENIC ARTERY WAS DAMAGED AND THE PROCEDURE WAS CONVERTED TO OPEN TO CONTROL THE BLEEDING. THE SURGEON WAS USING THE VESSEL SEALER EXTEND (VSE) INSTRUMENT AND MONOPOLAR CURVED SCISSORS (MCS) INSTRUMENT WHEN THE INJURY OCCURRED. THE SURGEON REPORTED HE WAS MOBILIZING THE ADRENAL GLAND WHICH WAS ABUTTING THE SPLENIC ARTERY. IN THE COURSE OF DISSECTING THE ADRENAL GLAND, THE SPLENIC ARTERY WAS INJURED. THE SURGEON REPORTED THAT THEY DO NOT THINK ANY DA VINCI PRODUCTS CAUSED OR CONTRIBUTED TO THE PATIENT INJURY. THE SURGEON OF THE PROCEDURE SAID THE CAUSE OF THE INJURY WAS THE CLOSE PROXIMITY OF THE ADRENAL GLAND AND THE SPLENIC ARTERY. THE SURGEON REPORTED THAT THE PATIENT HAD ABNORMAL ANATOMY AND THAT THEY ACCIDENTALLY NICKED THE SPLENIC ARTERY. THERE WERE SEVERE CALCIFICATIONS ON THE ARTERY AND THAT THE SLIGHT MANIPULATION CAUSED THE VESSEL TO BE INJURED. THE SURGEON SAID THIS COMPLICATION OCCURRED ABOUT 40 MINUTES INTO THE PROCEDURE. THE SURGEON REPORTED THAT 3,750CC OF BLOOD WAS LOST DUE TO THIS EVENT AND THAT BLOOD TRANSFUSIONS WERE ADMINISTERED. BEFORE CONVERTING, THE SURGEON ATTEMPTED TO CLAMP ON THE BLEEDING SPLENIC ARTERY, BUT DUE TO THE SEVERE CALCIFICATIONS, TORTUOSITY, AND BLEEDING, THE SURGEON WAS UNABLE TO GAIN CONTROL OF THE BLEEDING VESSEL. AFTER CONVERTING TO LAPAROTOMY, THE SPLENIC HILUM WAS PALPATED AND CLAMPS WERE APPLIED. DURING THE EVENT, THE PATIENT BECAME PULSELESS AND INTRA-OPERATIVE CPR WAS ADMINISTERED. WITH THE BLOOD TRANSFUSIONS AND CPR, THE PATIENT REGAINED A PULSE. THE PATIENT WAS LEFT OPEN AND RECEIVED ANOTHER OPERATION THE FOLLOWING DAY. AFTER THIS SECONDARY OPERATION, THE PATIENT DEVELOPED ISCHEMIA OF THE GREATER CURVATURE OF THE STOMACH WHICH REQUIRED IT TO BE EXCISED. THE SURGEON REPORTED THAT THE PROXIMAL ASPECT OF THE STOMACH REQUIRE EXCISION DUE TO ISCHEMIA AND A SMALL PERFORATION. THE PATIENT WAS IN HYPOVOLEMIC SHOCK AND REQUIRED MULTIPLE PRESSORS. THE PATIENT WAS HAVING DECREASING PRESSOR REQUIREMENTS AND WAS CLINICALLY IMPROVING, BUT THE PATIENT WAS UNABLE TO BE WEANED OFF FROM THE VENTILATION. THE PATIENTS FAMILY DECIDED TO DISCONTINUE SUPPORT FOR THE PATIENT AND LET HER PASS. THE SURGEON REPORTED THAT THE FINAL CAUSE OF DEATH WAS ACUTE RESPIRATORY FAILURE. THE PATIENT PASSED AWAY THE MORNING OF POST OPERATIVE DAY (POD) #5. THE SITE ROBOTICS COORDINATOR REPORTED THAT NEITHER THE MCS INSTRUMENT NOR THE VSE INSTRUMENT ARE AVAILABLE FOR RETURN.

BASED ON THE CURRENT INFORMATION PROVIDED, THE CAUSE OF THE PSC EVENT RESULTING IN DELAY IN THE OPEN PROCEDURE CAN BE POSSIBLY ATTRIBUTED TO USE ERROR. THE ROOT CAUSE OF THE DELAY IN MOVING THE PSC AWAY FROM THE OPERATIVE TABLE WAS THE RESULT OF IMPROPER UNDOCKING BY THE SURGICAL STAFF IN RELATION TO REMOVAL OF THE CANNULAS FROM THE USM. A PREVENTIVE MAINTENANCE (PM) OF THE SYSTEM WAS COMPLETED BY THE ISI FIELD SERVICE ENGINEER (FSE) AND PARTS WERE REPLACED PER ROUTINE MAINTENANCE. A FOLLOW-UP MDR WILL BE SUBMITTED ONCE THE INVESTIGATION IS COMPLETE. A REVIEW OF THE SYSTEM LOGS FOR THE PROCEDURE DATE OF (B)(6) 2022 WAS PERFORMED BY AN ISI TECHNICAL SUPPORT ENGINEER (TSE) AND THE FOLLOWING WAS OBSERVED: MULTIPLE PROCEDURES HAVE BEEN PERFORMED ON THIS SYSTEM AFTER THIS REPORTED COMPLAINT. THERE WERE NO RELEVANT ERRORS OBSERVED DURING THIS PROCEDURE THAT WOULD HAVE CAUSED ISSUES WITH UNDOCKING THE PSC. THE DA VINCI XI USER MANUAL PROVIDES INFORMATION FOR WHEN A SITUATION ARISES WHERE A CONVERSION TO OPEN SURGERY OR IMMEDIATE ACCESS TO THE PATIENT IS REQUIRED TO REMOVE THE SYSTEM FROM THE PATIENT. ACTIONS INCLUDE REMOVING THE INSTRUMENTS AND ENDOSCOPE FROM THE PATIENT, DISCONNECTING THE CANNULAE FROM THE ARMS, MOVING THE ARMS AWAY FROM THE PATIENT AND IF NECESSARY, MOVING THE PSC AWAY FROM THE PATIENT. A REVIEW OF THE EVENT WAS CONDUCTED BY AN ISI MEDICAL OFFICER AND THE FOLLOWING ADDITIONAL INFORMATION WAS PROVIDED: BASED ON THE INFORMATION IN THE DESCRIPTION OF EVENTS, A NUMBER OF ISSUES MAY HAVE CONTRIBUTED TO THIS CATASTROPHIC EVENT INCLUDING CHALLENGING ANATOMY WITH ADHESIONS, DIFFICULTY IN RECOGNIZING NEARBY ANATOMY AND THE INADVERTENT INJURY OR INADEQUATE SEAL TO THE SPLENIC ARTERY WHICH THEN LED TO MASSIVE BLEEDING. ONCE THIS INJURY OCCURRED, INABILITY TO UNDOCK AND REMOVE THE DA VINCI SYSTEM FURTHER HINDERED ADEQUATE PATIENT ACCESS AND LIKELY CONTRIBUTED TO ADDITIONAL BLOOD LOSS. THIS COMPLAINT IS BEING REPORTED DUE TO THE FOLLOWING CONCLUSION: DURING A DA VINCI-ASSISTED HIATAL HERNIA-PARAOESOPHAGEAL PROCEDURE THE SURGEON DID NOT SEE THAT PART OF THE SPLENIC ARTERY WAS IN THE JAWS OF THE VSE INSTRUMENT WHILE HE WAS DISSECTING OMENTUM WITH THE INSTRUMENT. HALF OF THE SPLENIC ARTERY WAS TRANSECTED AS A RESULT AND MASSIVE BLEEDING WAS SUBSEQUENTLY OBSERVED. IN ORDER TO CONTROL THE BLEEDING, THE SITE HAD TO CONVERT TO OPEN SURGERY. NOTE: REFER TO MEDWATCH REPORT (MDR) WITH PATIENT IDENTIFIER # 547816 FOR MDR SUBMISSION OF THE ADVERSE EVENT AND PRODUCT PROBLEM ASSOCIATED WITH THE VSE INSTRUMENT. IT WAS REPORTED BY THE INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC. (ISI) CLINICAL SALES REPRESENTATIVE (CSR) WHO WAS INFORMED BY A NURSE EDUCATOR (WHO HEARD ABOUT THE EVENT FROM A MORBIDITY AND MORTALITY ROUNDS; THE NURSE AND CSR WERE NOT PRESENT DURING THE PROCEDURE), THAT DURING A DA VINCI-ASSISTED HIATAL HERNIA-PARAOESOPHAGEAL PROCEDURE, AS THE SURGEON WAS DISSECTING OMENTUM WITH THE VESSEL SEALER EXTEND (VSE), HE DID NOT SEE THAT PART OF THE SPLENIC ARTERY WAS IN THE JAWS OF THE INSTRUMENT AND A RESULT PART OF THE SPLENIC ARTERY WAS TRANSECTED. THE SPLENIC ARTERY SUBSEQUENTLY BURST RESULTING IN MASSIVE BLEEDING. WHEN ATTEMPTING TO UNDOCK THE ROBOT FOR CONVERSION TO AN OPEN PROCEDURE, THE INSTRUMENTS WERE REMOVED AND THE CANNULAS WERE PULLED FROM THE PATIENT; HOWEVER, THE CANNULAS WERE NOT REMOVED FROM THE UNIVERSAL SURGICAL MANIPULATORS (USM). THE SITE TRIED TO MOVE THE PATIENT SIDE CART (PSC) AWAY FROM THE PATIENT, BUT THE CART WOULD NOT MOVE AS THE CANNULAS WERE STILL ATTACHED TO THE USMS. THE PSC WAS STILL DOCKED NEXT TO THE PATIENT RESULTING IN A DELAY IN THE PROCEDURE AS THE RESIDENT COULD NOT GET ON THAT SIDE OF THE TABLE TO ASSIST THE SURGEON. AFTER A FEW MINUTES, A NURSE THEN PUT THE PSC FROM DRIVE TO NEUTRAL AND MOVED THE PSC AWAY FROM THE PATIENT. THE PATIENT SUBSEQUENTLY DIED ON THE TABLE. IT WAS REPORTED THAT THE PATIENT HAD A LOT OF ADHESIONS AND WAS A HIGH RISK PATIENT WITH LOW BLOOD RESERVES. THE CSR WAS INFORMED THAT THE OMENTUM WAS NOTED TO BE THICK AND THERE WAS NO ENTRAPMENT OF TISSUE OR TISSUE STICKING TO THE JAWS OF THE VSE. ADDITIONALLY, THERE WAS NO REPORT OF ANY ISSUES WITH THE VSE DURING THE PROCEDURE AND NO VISUALIZATION ISSUES WITH THE ENDOSCOPE. THE GENERATOR USED WAS AN ERBE VIO DV GENERATOR. IT IS UNKNOWN IF THE VSE WAS RETAINED FOR ANALYSIS. ISI MADE MULTIPLE FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPTS TO OBTAIN ADDITIONAL INFORMATION FROM THE SURGEON. HOWEVER, NO FURTHER DETAILS HAVE BEEN RECEIVED AS OF THE DATE OF THIS REPORT. THE USER FACILITY RISK MANAGER INFORMED ISI THAT THEY WOULD NOT BE PROVIDING ANY ADDITIONAL INFORMATION .
AN INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC. (ISI) FIELD SERVICE ENGINEER (FSE) WAS DISPATCHED TO THE CUSTOMER SITE TO FURTHER INVESTIGATE THE REPORTED COMPLAINT. THREE UNIVERSAL SURGICAL MANIPULATOR (USM)S AND THE VIDEO PROCESSOR WERE REPLACED TO CORRECT PROBLEMS FOUND DURING A PREVENTIVE MAINTENANCE. THE SYSTEM WAS TESTED AND VERIFIED AS READY FOR USE. FAILURE ANALYSIS OF THE PRODUCTS REPLACED WERE COMPLETED AND THE FOLLOWING FINDINGS WERE OBTAINED: THE VIDEO PROCESSOR WAS INSTALLED AND TESTED IN THE TEST SYSTEM. AN ERROR 417 WAS LOGGED, INDICATING ONE OF THE REDUNDANT POWER SUPPLIES HAS FAILED. THE USM UNIT 1 WAS RETURNED FOR FAILURE ANALYSIS AND THE REPORTED FAILURE 1126 WAS CONFIRMED AND REPLICATED. WHEN THE ARM WAS TESTED ON THE TEST SYSTEM, IT FAILED NORMAL MODE AS IT TRIGGERED AN ERROR 319/1126. DURING A PATIENT SIDE CART (PSC) FIXTURE TEST PLATFORM (PFTP) TEST, THE USM FAILED A FIBER TEST POINTING TO THE ROLLING LOOP FIBER. WHEN THE ORIGINAL ROLLING LOOP FIBER WAS REPLACED WITH A GOLD ROLLING LOOP FIBER, THE ARM PASSED THE FIBER TEST. ALL OTHER PFTP TESTS PASSED EXCEPT YAW SENSOR CHECK AND PITCH FRICTION SPEED. AS A FIX TO THE REPORTED PROBLEM, THE ROLLING LOOP ASSEMBLY WILL BE REPLACED. THE USM UNIT 2 WAS RETURNED FOR FAILURE ANALYSIS AND PASSED NORMAL MODE. DURING VISUAL INSPECTION, VIBRATION COULD BE FELT WHEN BACK DRIVING THE UNIT. THE UNIT ALSO FAILED ON A PFTP AS IT FAILED THE PITCH FRICTION SPEED VERY SLOW TESTS. THE UNIT PASSED ALL THE OTHER TESTS. THE PITCH MOTOR MODULE, PITCH HARMONIC DRIVE, AND ELBOW BEARINGS WILL BE REPLACED AS A FIX TO THE REPORTED PROBLEM. THE USM UNIT 3 WAS RETURNED FOR FAILURE ANALYSIS AND PASSED NORMAL MODE. THE SYSTEM LOGGED ERRORS 1126 & 31226. THE UNIT WAS TESTED ON A PFTP, AND IT FAILED FIBER TESTS POINTING TO THE ROLLING LOOP. WHEN THE ROLLING LOOP FIBER WAS SWAPPED WITH A GOLD ONE, THE ERROR NO LONGER OCCURRED. THE UNIT WAS THEN TESTED THROUGH ON A PFTP (USING GOLD ROLLING LOOP FIBER) AND PASSED ALL REQUIRED TESTS, EXCEPT YAW SENSORS CHECK AND PITCH FRICTION. THE ROLLING LOOP FIBER ASSEMBLY WILL BE REPLACED AS A FIX TO THE REPORTED PROBLEM. THE ISI CLINICAL SALES REPRESENTATIVE (CSR) CONDUCTED TRAINING FOR EMERGENCY ROBOT UNDOCKING FOR A NUMBER OF HEALTH CARE PRACTITIONERS (HCPS) IN THE HOSPITAL FOLLOWING THE EVENT. TRAINING INCLUDED RETURN DEMONSTRATION OF URGENT UNDOCKING. ON 11-OCT-2022, AN ISI MEDICAL SAFETY OFFICER (MSO) SPOKE TO ISI CSR AND OBTAINED THE FOLLOWING INFORMATION: THE DECISION TO CONVERT TO OPEN SURGERY WAS IMMEDIATE. THE CSR IS UNSURE HOW THE UNDOCKING ISSUE CONTRIBUTED TO ACCESS BUT HE SAID THE CUSTOMER OPENED IMMEDIATELY.
REFER TO H10/H11 FOR FOLLOW-UP INFORMATION.
